file.content:
# Slice: ActiveTaskList

## Pattern

**View** (State View / Read Model)

## Summary

A read model that projects all events into an ordered list of active
(incomplete, non-deleted) tasks. This powers the main view of the application.

## Diagram

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    E1["TaskAdded"]:::event
    E2["TaskCompleted"]:::event
    E3["TaskReopened"]:::event
    E4["TaskDescriptionChanged"]:::event
    E5["TaskReprioritized"]:::event
    E6["TaskDeleted"]:::event
    VIEW["ActiveTaskList"]:::view

    E1 --> VIEW
    E2 --> VIEW
    E3 --> VIEW
    E4 --> VIEW
    E5 --> VIEW
    E6 --> VIEW

    classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff
    classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff
```

## Read Model: ActiveTaskList

### Fields

| Field | Type | Source Event(s) | Description |
|-------|------|----------------|-------------|
| tasks | list | (all events) | Ordered list of active tasks |
| tasks[].task_id | identifier | TaskAdded.task_id | Unique task identifier |
| tasks[].description | text | TaskAdded.description, TaskDescriptionChanged.description | Current description (latest value) |
| tasks[].position | number | TaskAdded.position, TaskReprioritized.position, TaskReopened (top) | Current priority position |
| completed_count | number | TaskCompleted (+1), TaskReopened (-1), TaskDeleted (if completed, -1) | Count of completed tasks for badge |

### Filters

- Only tasks where **completed = false** AND **deleted = false**.

### Ordering

- By position ascending. Position 1 = highest priority (top of list).

### Event Processing Rules

| Event | Effect on Read Model |
|-------|---------------------|
| TaskAdded | Add new task at the bottom of the list (highest position number) |
| TaskCompleted | Remove task from active list. Increment completed_count. |
| TaskReopened | Add task back to active list at position 1 (top). Shift existing tasks down. Decrement completed_count. |
| TaskDescriptionChanged | Update description of matching task. |
| TaskReprioritized | Move task to new position. Shift other tasks to accommodate. |
| TaskDeleted | Remove task from active list. If task was completed, decrement completed_count. |

### Information Completeness Check

| Display Element | Source |
|----------------|--------|
| Task description text | TaskAdded.description / TaskDescriptionChanged.description |
| Task order | TaskAdded.position / TaskReprioritized.position / TaskReopened (top) |
| Completed count badge | TaskCompleted / TaskReopened / TaskDeleted |
| Empty state message | Derived: tasks list is empty |

All fields trace to events. No missing data.

## Wireframe

```
+--------------------------------------------------+
|  My Tasks                        [Completed (3)]  |
+--------------------------------------------------+
|                                                    |
|  [ What needs to be done?            ] [+ Add]    |
|                                                    |
|  ::  Buy groceries                    [v] [ x ]   |
|  ::  Call the dentist                 [v] [ x ]   |
|  ::  Fix the leaky faucet            [v] [ x ]   |
|  ::  Write thank-you notes           [v] [ x ]   |
|                                                    |
+--------------------------------------------------+
```

## GWT Scenarios

### Scenario: TaskAdded adds task to the bottom of an empty list

**Given** (current projection state):
- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [], completed_count: 0 }

**When** (event to process):
- TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }

**Then** (resulting projection state):
- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }], completed_count: 0 }

---

### Scenario: TaskAdded adds task to the bottom of a populated list

**Given** (current projection state):
- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 }], completed_count: 0 }

**When** (event to process):
- TaskAdded { task_id: "task-003", description: "Fix the leaky faucet", position: 3 }

**Then** (resulting projection state):
- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 }, { task_id: "task-003", description: "Fix the leaky faucet", position: 3 }], completed_count: 0 }

---

### Scenario: TaskCompleted removes task from active list and increments completed count

**Given** (current projection state):
- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 }], completed_count: 1 }

**When** (event to process):
- TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-002" }

**Then** (resulting projection state):
- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }], completed_count: 2 }

---

### Scenario: TaskReopened adds task to top of list and decrements completed count

**Given** (current projection state):
- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, { task_id: "task-003", description: "Fix the leaky faucet", position: 2 }], completed_count: 2 }

**When** (event to process):
- TaskReopened { task_id: "task-002" }

**Then** (resulting projection state):
- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 1 }, { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 2 }, { task_id: "task-003", description: "Fix the leaky faucet", position: 3 }], completed_count: 1 }

**Note:** The reopened task's description ("Call the dentist") must be resolved from prior events. Existing tasks shift down (positions increment by 1).

---

### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged updates description of an active task

**Given** (current projection state):
- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist", position: 1 }], completed_count: 0 }

**When** (event to process):
- TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office" }

**Then** (resulting projection state):
- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office", position: 1 }], completed_count: 0 }

---

### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged for a completed task does not affect active list

**Given** (current projection state):
- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }], completed_count: 1 }

**When** (event to process):
- TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist office" }

**Then** (resulting projection state):
- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }], completed_count: 1 }

---

### Scenario: TaskReprioritized moves task to a new position (higher priority)

**Given** (current projection state):
- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 }, { task_id: "task-003", description: "Fix the leaky faucet", position: 3 }], completed_count: 0 }

**When** (event to process):
- TaskReprioritized { task_id: "task-003", position: 1 }

**Then** (resulting projection state):
- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-003", description: "Fix the leaky faucet", position: 1 }, { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 2 }, { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 3 }], completed_count: 0 }

---

### Scenario: TaskDeleted removes an active task from the list

**Given** (current projection state):
- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 }], completed_count: 1 }

**When** (event to process):
- TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-002" }

**Then** (resulting projection state):
- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }], completed_count: 1 }

---

### Scenario: TaskDeleted for a completed task decrements completed count but does not change active list

**Given** (current projection state):
- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }], completed_count: 2 }

**When** (event to process):
- TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-005" }

**Then** (resulting projection state):
- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }], completed_count: 1 }

**Note:** The projection must track whether a deleted task was completed in order to correctly adjust the completed_count.

1→# Slice: CompletedTaskList
     2→
     3→## Pattern
     4→
     5→**View** (State View / Read Model)
     6→
     7→## Summary
     8→
     9→A read model that projects events into a list of completed (done, non-deleted)
    10→tasks. This powers the completed tasks view where users can review finished work
    11→and reopen or delete tasks.
    12→
    13→## Diagram
    14→
    15→```mermaid
    16→flowchart LR
    17→    E1["TaskAdded"]:::event
    18→    E2["TaskCompleted"]:::event
    19→    E3["TaskReopened"]:::event
    20→    E4["TaskDescriptionChanged"]:::event
    21→    E6["TaskDeleted"]:::event
    22→    VIEW["CompletedTaskList"]:::view
    23→
    24→    E1 --> VIEW
    25→    E2 --> VIEW
    26→    E3 --> VIEW
    27→    E4 --> VIEW
    28→    E6 --> VIEW
    29→
    30→    classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff
    31→    classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff
    32→```
    33→
    34→## Read Model: CompletedTaskList
    35→
    36→### Fields
    37→
    38→| Field | Type | Source Event(s) | Description |
    39→|-------|------|----------------|-------------|
    40→| tasks | list | (filtered events) | List of completed tasks |
    41→| tasks[].task_id | identifier | TaskAdded.task_id | Unique task identifier |
    42→| tasks[].description | text | TaskAdded.description, TaskDescriptionChanged.description | Current description (latest value) |
    43→
    44→### Filters
    45→
    46→- Only tasks where **completed = true** AND **deleted = false**.
    47→
    48→### Ordering
    49→
    50→- By completion time descending (most recently completed first). Completion
    51→  time is derived from the TaskCompleted event timestamp.
    52→
    53→### Event Processing Rules
    54→
    55→| Event | Effect on Read Model |
    56→|-------|---------------------|
    57→| TaskAdded | No effect (new tasks are not completed). |
    58→| TaskCompleted | Add task to completed list. |
    59→| TaskReopened | Remove task from completed list. |
    60→| TaskDescriptionChanged | Update description of matching task (if present in list). |
    61→| TaskDeleted | Remove task from completed list (if present). |
    62→
    63→### Information Completeness Check
    64→
    65→| Display Element | Source |
    66→|----------------|--------|
    67→| Task description text (with strikethrough) | TaskAdded.description / TaskDescriptionChanged.description |
    68→| Task ordering (most recent first) | TaskCompleted event timestamp |
    69→| Empty state | Derived: tasks list is empty |
    70→
    71→All fields trace to events. No missing data.
    72→
    73→## Wireframe
    74→

# Slice: CompletedTaskList

## Pattern

**View** (State View / Read Model)

## Summary

A read model that projects events into a list of completed (done, non-deleted)
tasks. This powers the completed tasks view where users can review finished work
and reopen or delete tasks.

## Diagram

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    E1["TaskAdded"]:::event
    E2["TaskCompleted"]:::event
    E3["TaskReopened"]:::event
    E4["TaskDescriptionChanged"]:::event
    E6["TaskDeleted"]:::event
    VIEW["CompletedTaskList"]:::view

    E1 --> VIEW
    E2 --> VIEW
    E3 --> VIEW
    E4 --> VIEW
    E6 --> VIEW

    classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff
    classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff
```

## Read Model: CompletedTaskList

### Fields

| Field | Type | Source Event(s) | Description |
|-------|------|----------------|-------------|
| tasks | list | (filtered events) | List of completed tasks |
| tasks[].task_id | identifier | TaskAdded.task_id | Unique task identifier |
| tasks[].description | text | TaskAdded.description, TaskDescriptionChanged.description | Current description (latest value) |

### Filters

- Only tasks where **completed = true** AND **deleted = false**.

### Ordering

- By completion time descending (most recently completed first). Completion
  time is derived from the TaskCompleted event timestamp.

### Event Processing Rules

| Event | Effect on Read Model |
|-------|---------------------|
| TaskAdded | No effect (new tasks are not completed). |
| TaskCompleted | Add task to completed list. |
| TaskReopened | Remove task from completed list. |
| TaskDescriptionChanged | Update description of matching task (if present in list). |
| TaskDeleted | Remove task from completed list (if present). |

### Information Completeness Check

| Display Element | Source |
|----------------|--------|
| Task description text (with strikethrough) | TaskAdded.description / TaskDescriptionChanged.description |
| Task ordering (most recent first) | TaskCompleted event timestamp |
| Empty state | Derived: tasks list is empty |

All fields trace to events. No missing data.

## Wireframe

```
+--------------------------------------------------+
|  [< Back to Tasks]              Completed Tasks   |
+--------------------------------------------------+
|                                                    |
|  ~~Pick up dry cleaning~~       [Reopen]  [ x ]   |
|  ~~Send the invoice~~           [Reopen]  [ x ]   |
|  ~~Order new lightbulbs~~       [Reopen]  [ x ]   |
|                                                    |
+--------------------------------------------------+
```

## GWT Scenarios

### Scenario: TaskAdded has no effect on completed list

**Given** (current projection state):
- CompletedTaskList { tasks: [] }

**When** (event to process):
- TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }

**Then** (resulting projection state):
- CompletedTaskList { tasks: [] }

---

### Scenario: TaskCompleted adds task to the completed list

**Given** (current projection state):
- CompletedTaskList { tasks: [] }

**When** (event to process):
- TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-001" }

**Then** (resulting projection state):
- CompletedTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }] }

**Note:** The task's description must be resolved from prior events (TaskAdded or TaskDescriptionChanged).

---

### Scenario: TaskCompleted adds most recent completion to the top

**Given** (current projection state):
- CompletedTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }] }

**When** (event to process):
- TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-002" }

**Then** (resulting projection state):
- CompletedTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist" }, { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }] }

**Note:** Most recently completed task appears first (descending completion time).

---

### Scenario: TaskReopened removes task from completed list

**Given** (current projection state):
- CompletedTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist" }, { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }] }

**When** (event to process):
- TaskReopened { task_id: "task-002" }

**Then** (resulting projection state):
- CompletedTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }] }

---

### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged updates description of a completed task

**Given** (current projection state):
- CompletedTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }] }

**When** (event to process):
- TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries and snacks" }

**Then** (resulting projection state):
- CompletedTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries and snacks" }] }

---

### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged for a non-completed task does not affect completed list

**Given** (current projection state):
- CompletedTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }] }

**When** (event to process):
- TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-003", description: "Fix the leaky faucet soon" }

**Then** (resulting projection state):
- CompletedTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }] }

---

### Scenario: TaskDeleted removes task from completed list

**Given** (current projection state):
- CompletedTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist" }, { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }] }

**When** (event to process):
- TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-001" }

**Then** (resulting projection state):
- CompletedTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist" }] }

---

### Scenario: TaskDeleted for an active (non-completed) task does not affect completed list

**Given** (current projection state):
- CompletedTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }] }

**When** (event to process):
- TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-003" }

**Then** (resulting projection state):
- CompletedTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }] }

# Slice: AddTask ## Pattern **Command** (State Change) ## Summary The user types a task description and submits it. A new task is created at the bottom of the active list with the given description and a default position. ## Diagram ```mermaid flowchart LR UI["Active Task List
input field + Add button"] CMD["AddTask"]:::command EVT["TaskAdded"]:::event VIEW["ActiveTaskList"]:::view UI -->|"description"| CMD CMD --> EVT EVT --> VIEW classDef command fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff ``` ## Command: AddTask | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | description | text | What needs to be done (non-empty) | **Triggered by:** User types in the input field and clicks [+ Add] or presses Enter. **Rejection conditions:** - Description is empty or blank. ## Event: TaskAdded | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | Unique identifier assigned to the new task | | description | text | The task description provided by the user | | position | number | Position in the list (bottom / lowest priority) | ## Wireframe ``` +--------------------------------------------------+ | My Tasks [Completed (0)] | +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | [ Buy groceries_________________] [+ Add] | | ^--- user types here | | | | No tasks yet. Add one above! | | | +--------------------------------------------------+ After submitting: +--------------------------------------------------+ | My Tasks [Completed (0)] | +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | [ What needs to be done? ] [+ Add] | | | | :: Buy groceries [v] [ x ] | | | +--------------------------------------------------+ ``` ## Read Model Impact **ActiveTaskList:** New task appears at the bottom of the list. ## GWT Scenarios ### Scenario: Add task to an empty list **Given** (prior events): - (none -- no tasks exist yet) **When** (command): - AddTask { description: "Buy groceries" } **Then** (events produced): - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 } --- ### Scenario: Add task to a list with existing tasks (new task goes to bottom) **Given** (prior events): - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 } - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 } **When** (command): - AddTask { description: "Fix the leaky faucet" } **Then** (events produced): - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-003", description: "Fix the leaky faucet", position: 3 } --- ### Scenario: Add task when some tasks have been completed (position based on active + completed count) **Given** (prior events): - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 } - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 } - TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-001" } **When** (command): - AddTask { description: "Write thank-you notes" } **Then** (events produced): - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-004", description: "Write thank-you notes", position: 3 } **Note:** Empty/blank description is prevented by the type system (NonEmptyString domain type), not by command rejection. No GWT scenario is needed for that case.

defmodule TodoListWeb.CoreComponents do @moduledoc """ Provides core UI components. At first glance, this module may seem daunting, but its goal is to provide core building blocks for your application, such as tables, forms, and inputs. The components consist mostly of markup and are well-documented with doc strings and declarative assigns. You may customize and style them in any way you want, based on your application growth and needs. The foundation for styling is Tailwind CSS, a utility-first CSS framework, augmented with daisyUI, a Tailwind CSS plugin that provides UI components and themes. Here are useful references: * [daisyUI](https://daisyui.com/docs/intro/) - a good place to get started and see the available components. * [Tailwind CSS](https://tailwindcss.com) - the foundational framework we build on. You will use it for layout, sizing, flexbox, grid, and spacing. * [Heroicons](https://heroicons.com) - see `icon/1` for usage. * [Phoenix.Component](https://hexdocs.pm/phoenix_live_view/Phoenix.Component.html) - the component system used by Phoenix. Some components, such as `<.link>` and `<.form>`, are defined there. """ use Phoenix.Component use Gettext, backend: TodoListWeb.Gettext alias Phoenix.LiveView.JS @doc """ Renders flash notices. ## Examples <.flash kind={:info} flash={@flash} /> <.flash kind={:info} phx-mounted={show("#flash")}>Welcome Back! """ attr :id, :string, doc: "the optional id of flash container" attr :flash, :map, default: %{}, doc: "the map of flash messages to display" attr :title, :string, default: nil attr :kind, :atom, values: [:info, :error], doc: "used for styling and flash lookup" attr :rest, :global, doc: "the arbitrary HTML attributes to add to the flash container" slot :inner_block, doc: "the optional inner block that renders the flash message" def flash(assigns) do assigns = assign_new(assigns, :id, fn -> "flash-#{assigns.kind}" end) ~H"""
hide("##{@id}")} role="alert" class="toast toast-top toast-end z-50" {@rest} >
<.icon :if={@kind == :info} name="hero-information-circle" class="size-5 shrink-0" /> <.icon :if={@kind == :error} name="hero-exclamation-circle" class="size-5 shrink-0" />

{@title}

{msg}

""" end @doc """ Renders a button with navigation support. ## Examples <.button>Send! <.button phx-click="go" variant="primary">Send! <.button navigate={~p"/"}>Home """ attr :rest, :global, include: ~w(href navigate patch method download name value disabled) attr :class, :any attr :variant, :string, values: ~w(primary) slot :inner_block, required: true def button(%{rest: rest} = assigns) do variants = %{"primary" => "btn-primary", nil => "btn-primary btn-soft"} assigns = assign_new(assigns, :class, fn -> ["btn", Map.fetch!(variants, assigns[:variant])] end) if rest[:href] || rest[:navigate] || rest[:patch] do ~H""" <.link class={@class} {@rest}> {render_slot(@inner_block)} """ else ~H""" """ end end @doc """ Renders an input with label and error messages. A `Phoenix.HTML.FormField` may be passed as argument, which is used to retrieve the input name, id, and values. Otherwise all attributes may be passed explicitly. ## Types This function accepts all HTML input types, considering that: * You may also set `type="select"` to render a ` """ end def input(%{type: "checkbox"} = assigns) do assigns = assign_new(assigns, :checked, fn -> Phoenix.HTML.Form.normalize_value("checkbox", assigns[:value]) end) ~H"""
<.error :for={msg <- @errors}>{msg}
""" end def input(%{type: "select"} = assigns) do ~H"""
<.error :for={msg <- @errors}>{msg}
""" end def input(%{type: "textarea"} = assigns) do ~H"""
<.error :for={msg <- @errors}>{msg}
""" end # All other inputs text, datetime-local, url, password, etc. are handled here... def input(assigns) do ~H"""
<.error :for={msg <- @errors}>{msg}
""" end # Helper used by inputs to generate form errors defp error(assigns) do ~H"""

<.icon name="hero-exclamation-circle" class="size-5" /> {render_slot(@inner_block)}

""" end @doc """ Renders a header with title. """ slot :inner_block, required: true slot :subtitle slot :actions def header(assigns) do ~H"""

{render_slot(@inner_block)}

{render_slot(@subtitle)}

{render_slot(@actions)}
""" end @doc """ Renders a table with generic styling. ## Examples <.table id="users" rows={@users}> <:col :let={user} label="id">{user.id} <:col :let={user} label="username">{user.username} """ attr :id, :string, required: true attr :rows, :list, required: true attr :row_id, :any, default: nil, doc: "the function for generating the row id" attr :row_click, :any, default: nil, doc: "the function for handling phx-click on each row" attr :row_item, :any, default: &Function.identity/1, doc: "the function for mapping each row before calling the :col and :action slots" slot :col, required: true do attr :label, :string end slot :action, doc: "the slot for showing user actions in the last table column" def table(assigns) do assigns = with %{rows: %Phoenix.LiveView.LiveStream{}} <- assigns do assign(assigns, row_id: assigns.row_id || fn {id, _item} -> id end) end ~H"""
{col[:label]} {gettext("Actions")}
{render_slot(col, @row_item.(row))}
<%= for action <- @action do %> {render_slot(action, @row_item.(row))} <% end %>
""" end @doc """ Renders a data list. ## Examples <.list> <:item title="Title">{@post.title} <:item title="Views">{@post.views} """ slot :item, required: true do attr :title, :string, required: true end def list(assigns) do ~H"""
  • {item.title}
    {render_slot(item)}
""" end @doc """ Renders a [Heroicon](https://heroicons.com). Heroicons come in three styles – outline, solid, and mini. By default, the outline style is used, but solid and mini may be applied by using the `-solid` and `-mini` suffix. You can customize the size and colors of the icons by setting width, height, and background color classes. Icons are extracted from the `deps/heroicons` directory and bundled within your compiled app.css by the plugin in `assets/vendor/heroicons.js`. ## Examples <.icon name="hero-x-mark" /> <.icon name="hero-arrow-path" class="ml-1 size-3 motion-safe:animate-spin" /> """ attr :name, :string, required: true attr :class, :any, default: "size-4" def icon(%{name: "hero-" <> _} = assigns) do ~H""" """ end ## JS Commands def show(js \\ %JS{}, selector) do JS.show(js, to: selector, time: 300, transition: {"transition-all ease-out duration-300", "opacity-0 translate-y-4 sm:translate-y-0 sm:scale-95", "opacity-100 translate-y-0 sm:scale-100"} ) end def hide(js \\ %JS{}, selector) do JS.hide(js, to: selector, time: 200, transition: {"transition-all ease-in duration-200", "opacity-100 translate-y-0 sm:scale-100", "opacity-0 translate-y-4 sm:translate-y-0 sm:scale-95"} ) end @doc """ Translates an error message using gettext. """ def translate_error({msg, opts}) do # When using gettext, we typically pass the strings we want # to translate as a static argument: # # # Translate the number of files with plural rules # dngettext("errors", "1 file", "%{count} files", count) # # However the error messages in our forms and APIs are generated # dynamically, so we need to translate them by calling Gettext # with our gettext backend as first argument. Translations are # available in the errors.po file (as we use the "errors" domain). if count = opts[:count] do Gettext.dngettext(TodoListWeb.Gettext, "errors", msg, msg, count, opts) else Gettext.dgettext(TodoListWeb.Gettext, "errors", msg, opts) end end @doc """ Translates the errors for a field from a keyword list of errors. """ def translate_errors(errors, field) when is_list(errors) do for {^field, {msg, opts}} <- errors, do: translate_error({msg, opts}) end end

# Slice: ChangeTaskDescription ## Pattern **Command** (State Change) ## Summary The user clicks on a task's description text to activate inline editing, changes the text, and confirms the edit (by pressing Enter or clicking away). The task's description is updated. ## Diagram ```mermaid flowchart LR UI["Active Task List
inline edit field"] CMD["ChangeTaskDescription"]:::command EVT["TaskDescriptionChanged"]:::event VIEW1["ActiveTaskList"]:::view VIEW2["CompletedTaskList"]:::view UI -->|"task_id, new_description"| CMD CMD --> EVT EVT --> VIEW1 EVT --> VIEW2 classDef command fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff ``` ## Command: ChangeTaskDescription | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task to edit | | new_description | text | The updated description (non-empty) | **Triggered by:** User clicks on a task description in the active list, edits the text inline, and confirms by pressing Enter or blurring the field. **Rejection conditions:** - New description is empty or blank. - New description is identical to the current description (no change). ## Event: TaskDescriptionChanged | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task whose description changed | | description | text | The new description | ## Wireframe ``` Before (normal display): :: Call the dentist [v] [ x ] User clicks on the description text: :: [Call the dentist office____] [v] [ x ] ^--- inline editing active, cursor in text field User presses Enter or clicks away: :: Call the dentist office [v] [ x ] ^--- updated description displayed ``` ## Read Model Impact **ActiveTaskList:** Task description updated (if task is active). **CompletedTaskList:** Task description updated (if task is completed). ## GWT Scenarios ### Scenario: Change a task's description **Given** (prior events): - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist", position: 1 } **When** (command): - ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist office" } **Then** (events produced): - TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office" } --- ### Scenario: Reject changing description to the same value (no change) **Given** (prior events): - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist", position: 1 } **When** (command): - ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist" } **Then** (error -- no events): - Error: "Description is unchanged" --- ### Scenario: Change description after it was previously changed **Given** (prior events): - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist", position: 1 } - TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office" } **When** (command): - ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist office at 2pm" } **Then** (events produced): - TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office at 2pm" } --- ### Scenario: Reject changing description back to a previously changed value that matches current **Given** (prior events): - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist", position: 1 } - TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office" } **When** (command): - ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist office" } **Then** (error -- no events): - Error: "Description is unchanged" **Note:** Empty/blank description is prevented by the type system (NonEmptyString domain type), not by command rejection. No GWT scenario is needed for that case.

1→# Slice: CompleteTask 2→ 3→## Pattern 4→ 5→**Command** (State Change) 6→ 7→## Summary 8→ 9→The user clicks the checkbox on an active task to mark it as done. checkbox on task"] CMD["CompleteTask"]:::command EVT["TaskCompleted"]:::event VIEW1["ActiveTaskList"]:::view VIEW2["CompletedTaskList"]:::view UI -->|"task_id"| CMD CMD --> EVT EVT --> VIEW1 EVT --> VIEW2 classDef command fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff ``` ## Command: CompleteTask | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task to mark as complete | **Triggered by:** User clicks the [v] checkbox on an active task. **Rejection conditions:** - Task is already completed. ## Event: TaskCompleted | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task that was completed | ## Wireframe ``` Before: +--------------------------------------------------+ | My Tasks [Completed (2)] | +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | :: Buy groceries [v] [ x ] | | :: Call the dentist [v] [ x ] | | :: Fix the leaky faucet [v] [ x ] | | | +--------------------------------------------------+ User clicks [v] on "Call the dentist": +--------------------------------------------------+ | My Tasks [Completed (3)] | +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | :: Buy groceries [v] [ x ] | | :: Fix the leaky faucet [v] [ x ] | | | +--------------------------------------------------+ Task disappears from active list, completed count increments. ``` ## Read Model Impact **ActiveTaskList:** Task removed from active list. # Slice: DeleteTask ## Pattern **Command** (State Change) ## Summary The user clicks the delete button on a task (from either the active list or the completed tasks view). A confirmation dialog appears. If the user confirms, the task is permanently removed from all views. ## Diagram ```mermaid flowchart LR UI1["Active Task List
delete button"] UI2["Completed Tasks View
delete button"] DIALOG["Confirmation Dialog"] CMD["DeleteTask"]:::command EVT["TaskDeleted"]:::event VIEW1["ActiveTaskList"]:::view VIEW2["CompletedTaskList"]:::view UI1 -->|"click x"| DIALOG UI2 -->|"click x"| DIALOG DIALOG -->|"confirm, task_id"| CMD CMD --> EVT EVT --> VIEW1 EVT --> VIEW2 classDef command fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff ``` ## Command: DeleteTask | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task to delete | **Triggered by:** User clicks [x] on any task (active or completed), then clicks [Delete] in the confirmation dialog. **Rejection conditions:** - User clicks [Cancel] in the confirmation dialog (command is never issued). ## Event: TaskDeleted | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task that was deleted | **Business rule:** Deletion is permanent from the UI perspective. The event stream retains the full history, but the UI provides no way to restore a deleted task. ## Wireframe ``` User clicks [x] on "Fix the leaky faucet": +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | +--------------------------------------------+ | | | Delete Task? | | | | | | | | "Fix the leaky faucet" will be | | | | permanently removed. | | | | | | | | [Cancel] [Delete] | | | +--------------------------------------------+ | | | +--------------------------------------------------+ If user clicks [Delete], the task disappears from the list. If user clicks [Cancel], nothing changes. ``` ## Read Model Impact **ActiveTaskList:** Task removed (if it was active). Remaining tasks may shift positions. **CompletedTaskList:** Task removed (if it was completed). ## GWT Scenarios ### Scenario: Delete an active task **Given** (prior events): - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 } - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 } **When** (command): - DeleteTask { task_id: "task-002" } **Then** (events produced): - TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-002" } --- ### Scenario: Delete a completed task **Given** (prior events): - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 } - TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-001" } **When** (command): - DeleteTask { task_id: "task-001" } **Then** (events produced): - TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-001" } **Note:** The confirmation dialog is a UI concern. If the user clicks Cancel, the command is never issued, so there is no rejection scenario at the command level. Once the DeleteTask command is issued (after confirmation), it always succeeds.

Reopen button"] CMD["ReopenTask"]:::command EVT["TaskReopened"]:::event VIEW1["ActiveTaskList"]:::view VIEW2["CompletedTaskList"]:::view UI -->|"task_id"| CMD CMD --> EVT EVT --> VIEW1 EVT --> VIEW2 classDef command fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff ``` ## Command: ReopenTask | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The completed task to reopen | **Triggered by:** User clicks the [Reopen] button on a task in the completed tasks view. **Rejection conditions:** - Task is not completed (already active). ## Event: TaskReopened | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task that was reopened | **Business rule:** The reopened task is placed at the top of the active list (highest priority position). This differs from newly added tasks which go to the bottom. Rationale: if a user reopens a task, it is likely urgent. ## Wireframe ``` Before (Completed Tasks View): +--------------------------------------------------+ | [< Back to Tasks] Completed Tasks | +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | ~~Pick up dry cleaning~~ [Reopen] [ x ] | | ~~Send the invoice~~ [Reopen] [ x ] | | ~~Order new lightbulbs~~ [Reopen] [ x ] | | | +--------------------------------------------------+ User clicks [Reopen] on "Send the invoice": +--------------------------------------------------+ | [< Back to Tasks] Completed Tasks | +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | ~~Pick up dry cleaning~~ [Reopen] [ x ] | | ~~Order new lightbulbs~~ [Reopen] [ x ] | | | +--------------------------------------------------+ Active Task List now shows (navigating back): +--------------------------------------------------+ | My Tasks [Completed (2)] | +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | :: Send the invoice [v] [ x ] | | ^--- reopened task at TOP | | :: Buy groceries [v] [ x ] | | :: Fix the leaky faucet [v] [ x ] | | | +--------------------------------------------------+ ``` ## Read Model Impact **ActiveTaskList:** Task appears at the top (highest priority). Completed count decremented. **CompletedTaskList:** Task removed from completed list. ## GWT Scenarios ### Scenario: Reopen a completed task (goes to top of active list) **Given** (prior events): - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 } - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 } - TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-002" } **When** (command): - ReopenTask { task_id: "task-002" } **Then** (events produced): - TaskReopened { task_id: "task-002" } --- ### Scenario: Reject reopening a task that is already active **Given** (prior events): - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 } **When** (command): - ReopenTask { task_id: "task-001" } **Then** (error -- no events): - Error: "Task is not completed"

# Slice: ReprioritizeTask ## Pattern **Command** (State Change) ## Summary The user drags a task from one position in the active list and drops it at a new position. The task's priority changes relative to the other tasks. ## Diagram ```mermaid flowchart LR UI["Active Task List
drag handle"] CMD["ReprioritizeTask"]:::command EVT["TaskReprioritized"]:::event VIEW["ActiveTaskList"]:::view UI -->|"task_id, new_position"| CMD CMD --> EVT EVT --> VIEW classDef command fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff ``` ## Command: ReprioritizeTask | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task being moved | | new_position | number | The target position in the list | **Triggered by:** User grabs a drag handle (::) and drops the task at a new position in the active list. **Rejection conditions:** - Task dropped in the same position (no change). ## Event: TaskReprioritized | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task that was moved | | position | number | The new position in the priority-ordered list | ## Wireframe ``` Before: :: Buy groceries [v] [ x ] :: Call the dentist [v] [ x ] :: Fix the leaky faucet [v] [ x ] :: Write thank-you notes [v] [ x ] User drags "Fix the leaky faucet" to the top: :: Fix the leaky faucet [v] [ x ] <-- moved here :: Buy groceries [v] [ x ] :: Call the dentist [v] [ x ] :: Write thank-you notes [v] [ x ] ``` ## Read Model Impact **ActiveTaskList:** Task order updated. The moved task appears at its new position; other tasks shift to accommodate. ## GWT Scenarios ### Scenario: Move a task to a higher priority (lower position number) **Given** (prior events): - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 } - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 } - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-003", description: "Fix the leaky faucet", position: 3 } - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-004", description: "Write thank-you notes", position: 4 } **When** (command): - ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-003", new_position: 1 } **Then** (events produced): - TaskReprioritized { task_id: "task-003", position: 1 } --- ### Scenario: Move a task to a lower priority (higher position number) **Given** (prior events): - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 } - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 } - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-003", description: "Fix the leaky faucet", position: 3 } **When** (command): - ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-001", new_position: 3 } **Then** (events produced): - TaskReprioritized { task_id: "task-001", position: 3 } --- ### Scenario: Reject reprioritizing to the same position (no change) **Given** (prior events): - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 } - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 } **When** (command): - ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-002", new_position: 2 } **Then** (error -- no events): - Error: "Task is already at this position"

content.thinking:
Now I have a thorough understanding of the full picture. Let me create the Atomic Design document. Here's what I need to organize:

## The Atomic Design Hierarchy for this Todo App

### Atoms (smallest building blocks)
- Icon (already exists in core_components)
- DragHandle (the `::` affordance)
- Badge (the completed count)
- TaskDescriptionText (plain text display of a task description)
- StrikethroughText (completed task description)
- EmptyStateText ("No tasks yet. Add one above!")

### Molecules (groups of atoms forming simple UI units)
- TaskInput (text input + add button for new task form)


- InlineEditField (text field for editing task description in-place)
- CompleteCheckbox (the [v] checkbox on active tasks)
- ReopenButton (the [Reopen] button on completed tasks)
- DeleteButton (the [x] button on both views)
- ConfirmDeleteDialog (modal with Cancel/Delete buttons)
- NavigationLink (the "< Back to Tasks" and "Completed (N)" links)

### Organisms (complex UI components composed of molecules)
- ActiveTaskItem (drag handle + description + complete checkbox + delete button)
- CompletedTaskItem (strikethrough description + reopen button + delete button)
- TaskForm (text input + add button)
- ActiveTaskList (ordered list of ActiveTaskItems)
- CompletedTaskList (ordered list of CompletedTaskItems)
- PageHeader (title + navigation/badge)

### Templates (page-level layout without real data)
- ActiveTasksTemplate (header + add form + task list + empty state)
- CompletedTasksTemplate (header with back link + completed list + empty state)

### Pages (templates with real data)
- ActiveTasksPage (LiveView: TaskLive.Index)
- CompletedTasksPage (LiveView: TaskLive.Completed)

Now I'm thinking about how this maps to Phoenix LiveView architecture. In LiveView, the smaller molecules and atoms would live as function components in a shared module, while organisms become stateless function components that compose those pieces together, and the templates and pages map directly to LiveView modules that manage the actual state and data.

So I'd organize this as task-specific components in one file, then have the two main LiveView modules handle the ActiveTasks and CompletedTasks pages respectively, with the existing core_components providing the foundational UI elements. Let me reconsider this through Brad Frost's atomic design lens more carefully.

In Phoenix, there's a natural tension between Atomic Design's strict file-per-level structure and the framework's preference for colocating related components. The convention tends to be keeping generic reusable pieces in CoreComponents, domain-specific ones in feature modules or inline in LiveViews, and stateful interactive components as LiveComponents.

I'll write this out as a comprehensive design system document in docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md that covers both the design philosophy and the atomic hierarchy, structured with an overview of the theme system followed by the full breakdown.

Now I'm mapping out the component inventory to show where each piece lives in the codebase, documenting the interaction patterns like drag-drop and inline editing, and explaining how the atomic levels translate to actual Phoenix component implementations.

A brain the size of a planet, fully applied to categorizing buttons and text fields into increasingly elaborate taxonomic levels. How thrilling.

Let me now create the design system document with the Atomic Design hierarchy. I'll use the `sdlc:file-updater` agent since this is a documentation file outside TDD scope.

Create a new file at `docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md`. This is the UI design system document for a Phoenix LiveView todo list application using Atomic Design principles (Brad Frost's methodology).

## Context

This is an event-sourced Phoenix/LiveView todo list app that uses:
- **Tailwind CSS + daisyUI** for styling
- **Heroicons** for icons
- **Phoenix LiveView streams** for efficient list rendering
- **PubSub** for real-time updates
- Light and dark theme support via `data-theme` attribute

The app has two views:
1. **Active Tasks** (`/`) — main view with add form, drag-reorderable task list
2. **Completed Tasks** (`/completed`) — list of done tasks with reopen/delete

## Existing Components (in `lib/todo_list_web/components/core_components.ex`)

These already exist and should be referenced, not recreated:
- `flash/1` — toast-style flash messages (info/error)
- `button/1` — button with navigation support, primary variant
- `input/1` — form inputs (text, select, textarea, checkbox) with error handling
- `header/1` — page header with title, subtitle, actions slots
- `icon/1` — Heroicons wrapper
- `show/2`, `hide/2` — JS transition helpers

Also existing in `lib/todo_list_web/components/layouts.ex`:
- `app/1` — app layout with navbar
- `flash_group/1` — flash message container
- `theme_toggle/1` — system/light/dark theme switcher

## Wireframes (from event model)

### Active Tasks View
```
+--------------------------------------------------+
|  My Tasks                        [Completed (3)]  |
+--------------------------------------------------+
|                                                    |
|  [ What needs to be done?            ] [+ Add]    |
|                                                    |
|  ::  Buy groceries                    [v] [ x ]   |
|  ::  Call the dentist                 [v] [ x ]   |
|  ::  Fix the leaky faucet            [v] [ x ]   |
|  ::  Write thank-you notes           [v] [ x ]   |
|                                                    |
+--------------------------------------------------+
```

Empty state:
```
|         No tasks yet. Add one above!               |
```

### Completed Tasks View
```
+--------------------------------------------------+
|  [< Back to Tasks]              Completed Tasks   |
+--------------------------------------------------+
|                                                    |
|  ~~Pick up dry cleaning~~       [Reopen]  [ x ]   |
|  ~~Send the invoice~~           [Reopen]  [ x ]   |
|  ~~Order new lightbulbs~~       [Reopen]  [ x ]   |
|                                                    |
+--------------------------------------------------+
```

### Inline Edit (ChangeTaskDescription)
```
Before:    ::  Call the dentist                 [v] [ x ]
Editing:   ::  [Call the dentist office____]    [v] [ x ]
After:     ::  Call the dentist office          [v] [ x ]
```

### Delete Confirmation Dialog
```
+--------------------------------------------+
|  Delete Task?                               |
|                                             |
|  "Fix the leaky faucet" will be             |
|  permanently removed.                       |
|                                             |
|           [Cancel]  [Delete]                |
+--------------------------------------------+
```

### Drag-and-Drop Reorder (ReprioritizeTask)
The `::` prefix is a drag handle. Users grab it to reorder tasks.

## Interactions Summary

| Interaction | Trigger | Component |
|---|---|---|
| Add task | Type + Enter or click [+ Add] | AddTaskForm |
| Complete task | Click checkbox [v] | ActiveTaskItem |
| Delete task | Click [x], confirm in dialog | DeleteButton + ConfirmDialog |
| Reopen task | Click [Reopen] | CompletedTaskItem |
| Edit description | Click description text → inline edit → Enter/blur | ActiveTaskItem |
| Reorder tasks | Drag handle (::) drop at new position | ActiveTaskList |
| Navigate to completed | Click [Completed (N)] badge | PageHeader |
| Navigate back to active | Click [< Back to Tasks] | PageHeader |

## Theme Configuration

CSS uses daisyUI themes with OKLch colors:
- **Dark theme**: Purple-tinted dark backgrounds, purple primary (`oklch(58% 0.233 277.117)`)
- **Light theme**: Clean white backgrounds, warm orange primary (`oklch(70% 0.213 47.604)`)
- Both themes share: `border-radius: 0.25rem` (fields), `0.5rem` (boxes), `1.5px` borders

## Write the Document

Create `docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md` with the following structure:

### 1. Overview
Brief intro: Brad Frost's Atomic Design applied to a Phoenix LiveView todo app. Reference the theme system. Note that generic components already exist in CoreComponents; this document defines the task-specific component hierarchy.

### 2. Atoms
The smallest, indivisible UI building blocks. Include:

- **Icon** — existing `CoreComponents.icon/1` (Heroicons). Used for: drag handle grip, checkmark, X/close, arrow-left, plus.
- **Badge** — small pill displaying a count. Used for: completed task count in header. daisyUI `badge` class.
- **TaskDescriptionText** — plain text rendering of a task description. Applies `text-base-content` styling.
- **StrikethroughText** — completed task description with `line-through` decoration.
- **EmptyStateMessage** — centered, muted text for when a list is empty (e.g., "No tasks yet. Add one above!" or "No completed tasks.").
- **DragHandle** — the `::` grip affordance (six-dot pattern or grip-vertical icon). Indicates draggable. `cursor-grab` / `cursor-grabbing` states.

### 3. Molecules
Small groups of atoms that function as a unit:

- **TaskInput** — text input + submit button for adding tasks. Composed of: `input` atom (text field with placeholder "What needs to be done?") + `button` atom ([+ Add]). Wrapped in a `<.form>` with `phx-submit`.
- **CompleteCheckbox** — clickable checkbox that fires `phx-click="complete_task"` with the task_id. Visual: unchecked square that completes on click.
- **ReopenButton** — text button reading "Reopen" that fires `phx-click="reopen_task"`. Styled as `btn-ghost btn-sm`.
- **DeleteButton** — icon button ([x]) that triggers the delete confirmation flow. `phx-click` opens modal. Styled as `btn-ghost btn-sm btn-error` (subtle).
- **ConfirmDeleteDialog** — modal/dialog with task description, warning text, [Cancel] + [Delete] buttons. Uses daisyUI `modal` component. [Delete] styled `btn-error`, [Cancel] styled `btn-ghost`.
- **InlineEditField** — text input that replaces TaskDescriptionText on click. Submits on Enter or blur (`phx-blur` + `phx-keydown`). Reverts on Escape.

### 4. Organisms
Complex, distinct sections of the UI composed of molecules:

- **AddTaskForm** — the full add-task row. Composed of: TaskInput molecule. Handles form submission, input clearing on success, error display on validation failure.
- **ActiveTaskItem** — a single row in the active task list. Composed of: DragHandle + TaskDescriptionText (or InlineEditField when editing) + CompleteCheckbox + DeleteButton. Has `phx-click` on description to enter edit mode. Draggable via handle. Rendered within a LiveView stream.
- **CompletedTaskItem** — a single row in the completed list. Composed of: StrikethroughText + ReopenButton + DeleteButton. Simpler than ActiveTaskItem (no drag, no inline edit, no checkbox).
- **ActiveTaskList** — ordered container of ActiveTaskItems. Uses `phx-update="stream"` for efficient DOM updates. Implements drag-and-drop reordering (via JS hook). Shows EmptyStateMessage when empty.
- **CompletedTaskList** — container of CompletedTaskItems. Uses `phx-update="stream"`. Shows EmptyStateMessage when empty.
- **TaskPageHeader** — header bar for task pages. Two variants:
  - Active view: "My Tasks" title + [Completed (N)] navigation badge (right-aligned)
  - Completed view: [< Back to Tasks] navigation link (left) + "Completed Tasks" title (right)

### 5. Templates
Page-level structural layouts (no real data):

- **ActiveTasksTemplate** — vertical layout: TaskPageHeader (active variant) → AddTaskForm → ActiveTaskList (or EmptyStateMessage). Max-width container, centered.
- **CompletedTasksTemplate** — vertical layout: TaskPageHeader (completed variant) → CompletedTaskList (or EmptyStateMessage). Max-width container, centered.

### 6. Pages
Templates populated with real data from LiveView assigns:

- **ActiveTasksPage** → `TodoListWeb.TaskLive.Index` — mounts at `/`. Subscribes to PubSub for real-time updates. Assigns: `@tasks` (stream), `@completed_count` (integer), `@form` (changeset for add task). Handles events: `add_task`, `complete_task`, `delete_task`, `change_description`, `reorder`.
- **CompletedTasksPage** → `TodoListWeb.TaskLive.Completed` — mounts at `/completed`. Subscribes to PubSub. Assigns: `@tasks` (stream). Handles events: `reopen_task`, `delete_task`.

### 7. Component-to-File Mapping

Create a table mapping each component to its file location:

| Level | Component | File |
|-------|-----------|------|
| Atom | Icon, Button, Input | `core_components.ex` (existing) |
| Atom | Badge, DragHandle, EmptyStateMessage, TaskDescriptionText, StrikethroughText | `task_components.ex` |
| Molecule | TaskInput, CompleteCheckbox, ReopenButton, DeleteButton, ConfirmDeleteDialog, InlineEditField | `task_components.ex` |
| Organism | AddTaskForm, ActiveTaskItem, CompletedTaskItem, ActiveTaskList, CompletedTaskList, TaskPageHeader | `task_components.ex` |
| Template | ActiveTasksTemplate, CompletedTasksTemplate | Inline in LiveView `render/1` |
| Page | ActiveTasksPage, CompletedTasksPage | `task_live/index.ex`, `task_live/completed.ex` |

File paths:
- `lib/todo_list_web/components/core_components.ex` — generic atoms (existing, don't modify)
- `lib/todo_list_web/components/task_components.ex` — task-specific atoms, molecules, organisms (NEW)
- `lib/todo_list_web/live/task_live/index.ex` — active tasks LiveView page (NEW)
- `lib/todo_list_web/live/task_live/completed.ex` — completed tasks LiveView page (NEW)

### 8. Interaction Patterns

Document these patterns:

**Drag-and-Drop Reordering:**
- Implemented via a Phoenix LiveView JS hook (e.g., `SortableHook` using SortableJS or native HTML5 drag API)
- DragHandle atom is the only grab target (`[draggable]`)
- On drop: hook pushes `reorder` event with `{task_id, new_position}` to LiveView
- LiveView dispatches `ReprioritizeTask` command
- Stream updates reflect new order

**Inline Editing:**
- Click on TaskDescriptionText → replaces with InlineEditField (phx-click on the text element)
- InlineEditField auto-focuses on mount
- Enter key → submit new description (phx-keydown)
- Blur → submit new description (phx-blur)  
- Escape → cancel editing, revert to display mode (phx-keydown)
- LiveView dispatches `ChangeTaskDescription` command on submit
- Optimistic: no client-side optimism; wait for event to propagate through projection

**Delete Confirmation:**
- Click DeleteButton → shows ConfirmDeleteDialog (JS.show or daisyUI modal toggle)
- Dialog displays task description in quotes
- [Cancel] → closes dialog (JS command, no server roundtrip)
- [Delete] → `phx-click="delete_task"` with task_id → server dispatches DeleteTask command → dialog closes on success

### 9. Accessibility Notes

- All interactive elements have visible focus indicators (daisyUI provides this)
- Delete confirmation prevents accidental deletions
- Drag handle has `aria-grabbed`, `aria-dropeffect` attributes
- Keyboard support: Enter/Escape in inline edit, Enter in add form
- Screen reader: task list is `role="list"`, tasks are `role="listitem"`
- Color contrast meets WCAG AA in both light and dark themes (daisyUI defaults)
- Empty states are announced (not just visual)

### 10. daisyUI Component Usage

Map daisyUI components to our design:

| Our Component | daisyUI Base | Customization |
|---|---|---|
| Badge | `badge badge-primary` | Shows count |
| TaskInput | `input input-bordered` | With join for input+button group |
| AddButton | `btn btn-primary` | In join group |
| CompleteCheckbox | `checkbox checkbox-primary checkbox-sm` | Unchecked state |
| ReopenButton | `btn btn-ghost btn-sm` | Text only |
| DeleteButton | `btn btn-ghost btn-sm` | Error color on hover |
| ConfirmDeleteDialog | `modal` + `modal-box` | Standard modal pattern |
| ActiveTaskItem | `flex items-center gap-2` | Custom flex row |
| TaskPageHeader | `flex justify-between items-center` | Custom header |

Keep the document focused and practical. No fluff. This should be a working reference for implementation.

|  ~~Pick up dry cleaning~~       [Reopen]  [ x ]   |
|  ~~Send the invoice~~           [Reopen]  [ x ]   |
|  ~~Order new lightbulbs~~       [Reopen]  [ x ]   |
|                                                    |
+--------------------------------------------------+
```

### Inline Edit (ChangeTaskDescription)
```
Before:    ::  Call the dentist                 [v] [ x ]
Editing:   ::  [Call the dentist office____]    [v] [ x ]
After:     ::  Call the dentist office          [v] [ x ]
```

### Delete Confirmation Dialog
```
+--------------------------------------------+
|  Delete Task?                               |
|                                             |
|  "Fix the leaky faucet" will be             |
|  permanently removed.                       |
|                                             |
|           [Cancel]  [Delete]                |
+--------------------------------------------+
```

### Drag-and-Drop Reorder (ReprioritizeTask)
The `::` prefix is a drag handle. Users grab it to reorder tasks.

## Interactions Summary

| Interaction | Trigger | Component |
|---|---|---|
| Add task | Type + Enter or click [+ Add] | AddTaskForm |
| Complete task | Click checkbox [v] | ActiveTaskItem |
| Delete task | Click [x], confirm in dialog | DeleteButton + ConfirmDialog |
| Reopen task | Click [Reopen] | CompletedTaskItem |
| Edit description | Click description text → inline edit → Enter/blur | ActiveTaskItem |
| Reorder tasks | Drag handle (::) drop at new position | ActiveTaskList |
| Navigate to completed | Click [Completed (N)] badge | PageHeader |
| Navigate back to active | Click [< Back to Tasks] | PageHeader |

## Theme Configuration

CSS uses daisyUI themes with OKLch colors:
- **Dark theme**: Purple-tinted dark backgrounds, purple primary (`oklch(58% 0.233 277.117)`)
- **Light theme**: Clean white backgrounds, warm orange primary (`oklch(70% 0.213 47.604)`)
- Both themes share: `border-radius: 0.25rem` (fields), `0.5rem` (boxes), `1.5px` borders

## Write the Document

Create `docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md` with the following structure:

### 1. Overview
Brief intro: Brad Frost's Atomic Design applied to a Phoenix LiveView todo app. Reference the theme system. Note that generic components already exist in CoreComponents; this document defines the task-specific component hierarchy.

### 2. Atoms
The smallest, indivisible UI building blocks. Include:

- **Icon** — existing `CoreComponents.icon/1` (Heroicons). Used for: drag handle grip, checkmark, X/close, arrow-left, plus.
- **Badge** — small pill displaying a count. Used for: completed task count in header. daisyUI `badge` class.
- **TaskDescriptionText** — plain text rendering of a task description. Applies `text-base-content` styling.
- **StrikethroughText** — completed task description with `line-through` decoration.
- **EmptyStateMessage** — centered, muted text for when a list is empty (e.g., "No tasks yet. Add one above!" or "No completed tasks.").
- **DragHandle** — the `::` grip affordance (six-dot pattern or grip-vertical icon). Indicates draggable. `cursor-grab` / `cursor-grabbing` states.

### 3. Molecules
Small groups of atoms that function as a unit:

- **TaskInput** — text input + submit button for adding tasks. Composed of: `input` atom (text field with placeholder "What needs to be done?") + `button` atom ([+ Add]). Wrapped in a `<.form>` with `phx-submit`.
- **CompleteCheckbox** — clickable checkbox that fires `phx-click="complete_task"` with the task_id. Visual: unchecked square that completes on click.
- **ReopenButton** — text button reading "Reopen" that fires `phx-click="reopen_task"`. Styled as `btn-ghost btn-sm`.
- **DeleteButton** — icon button ([x]) that triggers the delete confirmation flow. `phx-click` opens modal. Styled as `btn-ghost btn-sm btn-error` (subtle).
- **ConfirmDeleteDialog** — modal/dialog with task description, warning text, [Cancel] + [Delete] buttons. Uses daisyUI `modal` component. [Delete] styled `btn-error`, [Cancel] styled `btn-ghost`.
- **InlineEditField** — text input that replaces TaskDescriptionText on click. Submits on Enter or blur (`phx-blur` + `phx-keydown`). Reverts on Escape.

### 4. Organisms
Complex, distinct sections of the UI composed of molecules:

- **AddTaskForm** — the full add-task row. Composed of: TaskInput molecule. Handles form submission, input clearing on success, error display on validation failure.
- **ActiveTaskItem** — a single row in the active task list. Composed of: DragHandle + TaskDescriptionText (or InlineEditField when editing) + CompleteCheckbox + DeleteButton. Has `phx-click` on description to enter edit mode. Draggable via handle. Rendered within a LiveView stream.
- **CompletedTaskItem** — a single row in the completed list. Composed of: StrikethroughText + ReopenButton + DeleteButton. Simpler than ActiveTaskItem (no drag, no inline edit, no checkbox).
- **ActiveTaskList** — ordered container of ActiveTaskItems. Uses `phx-update="stream"` for efficient DOM updates. Implements drag-and-drop reordering (via JS hook). Shows EmptyStateMessage when empty.
- **CompletedTaskList** — container of CompletedTaskItems. Uses `phx-update="stream"`. Shows EmptyStateMessage when empty.
- **TaskPageHeader** — header bar for task pages. Two variants:
  - Active view: "My Tasks" title + [Completed (N)] navigation badge (right-aligned)
  - Completed view: [< Back to Tasks] navigation link (left) + "Completed Tasks" title (right)

### 5. Templates
Page-level structural layouts (no real data):

- **ActiveTasksTemplate** — vertical layout: TaskPageHeader (active variant) → AddTaskForm → ActiveTaskList (or EmptyStateMessage). Max-width container, centered.
- **CompletedTasksTemplate** — vertical layout: TaskPageHeader (completed variant) → CompletedTaskList (or EmptyStateMessage). Max-width container, centered.

### 6. Pages
Templates populated with real data from LiveView assigns:

- **ActiveTasksPage** → `TodoListWeb.TaskLive.Index` — mounts at `/`. Subscribes to PubSub for real-time updates. Assigns: `@tasks` (stream), `@completed_count` (integer), `@form` (changeset for add task). Handles events: `add_task`, `complete_task`, `delete_task`, `change_description`, `reorder`.
- **CompletedTasksPage** → `TodoListWeb.TaskLive.Completed` — mounts at `/completed`. Subscribes to PubSub. Assigns: `@tasks` (stream). Handles events: `reopen_task`, `delete_task`.

### 7. Component-to-File Mapping

Create a table mapping each component to its file location:

| Level | Component | File |
|-------|-----------|------|
| Atom | Icon, Button, Input | `core_components.ex` (existing) |
| Atom | Badge, DragHandle, EmptyStateMessage, TaskDescriptionText, StrikethroughText | `task_components.ex` |
| Molecule | TaskInput, CompleteCheckbox, ReopenButton, DeleteButton, ConfirmDeleteDialog, InlineEditField | `task_components.ex` |
| Organism | AddTaskForm, ActiveTaskItem, CompletedTaskItem, ActiveTaskList, CompletedTaskList, TaskPageHeader | `task_components.ex` |
| Template | ActiveTasksTemplate, CompletedTasksTemplate | Inline in LiveView `render/1` |
| Page | ActiveTasksPage, CompletedTasksPage | `task_live/index.ex`, `task_live/completed.ex` |

File paths:
- `lib/todo_list_web/components/core_components.ex` — generic atoms (existing, don't modify)
- `lib/todo_list_web/components/task_components.ex` — task-specific atoms, molecules, organisms (NEW)
- `lib/todo_list_web/live/task_live/index.ex` — active tasks LiveView page (NEW)
- `lib/todo_list_web/live/task_live/completed.ex` — completed tasks LiveView page (NEW)

### 8. Interaction Patterns

Document these patterns:

**Drag-and-Drop Reordering:**
- Implemented via a Phoenix LiveView JS hook (e.g., `SortableHook` using SortableJS or native HTML5 drag API)
- DragHandle atom is the only grab target (`[draggable]`)
- On drop: hook pushes `reorder` event with `{task_id, new_position}` to LiveView
- LiveView dispatches `ReprioritizeTask` command
- Stream updates reflect new order

**Inline Editing:**
- Click on TaskDescriptionText → replaces with InlineEditField (phx-click on the text element)
- InlineEditField auto-focuses on mount
- Enter key → submit new description (phx-keydown)
- Blur → submit new description (phx-blur)  
- Escape → cancel editing, revert to display mode (phx-keydown)
- LiveView dispatches `ChangeTaskDescription` command on submit
- Optimistic: no client-side optimism; wait for event to propagate through projection

**Delete Confirmation:**
- Click DeleteButton → shows ConfirmDeleteDialog (JS.show or daisyUI modal toggle)
- Dialog displays task description in quotes
- [Cancel] → closes dialog (JS command, no server roundtrip)
- [Delete] → `phx-click="delete_task"` with task_id → server dispatches DeleteTask command → dialog closes on success

### 9. Accessibility Notes

- All interactive elements have visible focus indicators (daisyUI provides this)
- Delete confirmation prevents accidental deletions
- Drag handle has `aria-grabbed`, `aria-dropeffect` attributes
- Keyboard support: Enter/Escape in inline edit, Enter in add form
- Screen reader: task list is `role="list"`, tasks are `role="listitem"`
- Color contrast meets WCAG AA in both light and dark themes (daisyUI defaults)
- Empty states are announced (not just visual)

### 10. daisyUI Component Usage

Map daisyUI components to our design:

| Our Component | daisyUI Base | Customization |
|---|---|---|
| Badge | `badge badge-primary` | Shows count |
| TaskInput | `input input-bordered` | With join for input+button group |
| AddButton | `btn btn-primary` | In join group |
| CompleteCheckbox | `checkbox checkbox-primary checkbox-sm` | Unchecked state |
| ReopenButton | `btn btn-ghost btn-sm` | Text only |
| DeleteButton | `btn btn-ghost btn-sm` | Error color on hover |
| ConfirmDeleteDialog | `modal` + `modal-box` | Standard modal pattern |
| ActiveTaskItem | `flex items-center gap-2` | Custom flex row |
| TaskPageHeader | `flex justify-between items-center` | Custom header |

Keep the document focused and practical. No fluff. This should be a working reference for implementation.

- **DeleteButton** — icon button ([x]) that triggers the delete confirmation flow. `phx-click` opens modal. Styled as `btn-ghost btn-sm btn-error` (subtle).
- **ConfirmDeleteDialog** — modal/dialog with task description, warning text, [Cancel] + [Delete] buttons. Uses daisyUI `modal` component. [Delete] styled `btn-error`, [Cancel] styled `btn-ghost`.
- **InlineEditField** — text input that replaces TaskDescriptionText on click. Submits on Enter or blur (`phx-blur` + `phx-keydown`). Reverts on Escape.

### 4. Organisms
Complex, distinct sections of the UI composed of molecules:

- **AddTaskForm** — the full add-task row. Composed of: TaskInput molecule. Handles form submission, input clearing on success, error display on validation failure.
- **ActiveTaskItem** — a single row in the active task list. Composed of: DragHandle + TaskDescriptionText (or InlineEditField when editing) + CompleteCheckbox + DeleteButton. Has `phx-click` on description to enter edit mode. Draggable via handle. Rendered within a LiveView stream.
- **CompletedTaskItem** — a single row in the completed list. Composed of: StrikethroughText + ReopenButton + DeleteButton. Simpler than ActiveTaskItem (no drag, no inline edit, no checkbox).
- **ActiveTaskList** — ordered container of ActiveTaskItems. Uses `phx-update="stream"` for efficient DOM updates. Implements drag-and-drop reordering (via JS hook). Shows EmptyStateMessage when empty.
- **CompletedTaskList** — container of CompletedTaskItems. Uses `phx-update="stream"`. Shows EmptyStateMessage when empty.
- **TaskPageHeader** — header bar for task pages. Two variants:
  - Active view: "My Tasks" title + [Completed (N)] navigation badge (right-aligned)
  - Completed view: [< Back to Tasks] navigation link (left) + "Completed Tasks" title (right)

### 5. Templates
Page-level structural layouts (no real data):

- **ActiveTasksTemplate** — vertical layout: TaskPageHeader (active variant) → AddTaskForm → ActiveTaskList (or EmptyStateMessage). Max-width container, centered.
- **CompletedTasksTemplate** — vertical layout: TaskPageHeader (completed variant) → CompletedTaskList (or EmptyStateMessage). Max-width container, centered.

### 6. Pages
Templates populated with real data from LiveView assigns:

- **ActiveTasksPage** → `TodoListWeb.TaskLive.Index` — mounts at `/`. Subscribes to PubSub for real-time updates. Assigns: `@tasks` (stream), `@completed_count` (integer), `@form` (changeset for add task). Handles events: `add_task`, `complete_task`, `delete_task`, `change_description`, `reorder`.
- **CompletedTasksPage** → `TodoListWeb.TaskLive.Completed` — mounts at `/completed`. Subscribes to PubSub. Assigns: `@tasks` (stream). Handles events: `reopen_task`, `delete_task`.

### 7. Component-to-File Mapping

Create a table mapping each component to its file location:

| Level | Component | File |
|-------|-----------|------|
| Atom | Icon, Button, Input | `core_components.ex` (existing) |
| Atom | Badge, DragHandle, EmptyStateMessage, TaskDescriptionText, StrikethroughText | `task_components.ex` |
| Molecule | TaskInput, CompleteCheckbox, ReopenButton, DeleteButton, ConfirmDeleteDialog, InlineEditField | `task_components.ex` |
| Organism | AddTaskForm, ActiveTaskItem, CompletedTaskItem, ActiveTaskList, CompletedTaskList, TaskPageHeader | `task_components.ex` |
| Template | ActiveTasksTemplate, CompletedTasksTemplate | Inline in LiveView `render/1` |
| Page | ActiveTasksPage, CompletedTasksPage | `task_live/index.ex`, `task_live/completed.ex` |

File paths:
- `lib/todo_list_web/components/core_components.ex` — generic atoms (existing, don't modify)
- `lib/todo_list_web/components/task_components.ex` — task-specific atoms, molecules, organisms (NEW)
- `lib/todo_list_web/live/task_live/index.ex` — active tasks LiveView page (NEW)
- `lib/todo_list_web/live/task_live/completed.ex` — completed tasks LiveView page (NEW)

### 8. Interaction Patterns

Document these patterns:

**Drag-and-Drop Reordering:**
- Implemented via a Phoenix LiveView JS hook (e.g., `SortableHook` using SortableJS or native HTML5 drag API)
- DragHandle atom is the only grab target (`[draggable]`)
- On drop: hook pushes `reorder` event with `{task_id, new_position}` to LiveView
- LiveView dispatches `ReprioritizeTask` command
- Stream updates reflect new order

**Inline Editing:**
- Click on TaskDescriptionText → replaces with InlineEditField (phx-click on the text element)
- InlineEditField auto-focuses on mount
- Enter key → submit new description (phx-keydown)
- Blur → submit new description (phx-blur)  
- Escape → cancel editing, revert to display mode (phx-keydown)
- LiveView dispatches `ChangeTaskDescription` command on submit
- Optimistic: no client-side optimism; wait for event to propagate through projection

**Delete Confirmation:**
- Click DeleteButton → shows ConfirmDeleteDialog (JS.show or daisyUI modal toggle)
- Dialog displays task description in quotes
- [Cancel] → closes dialog (JS command, no server roundtrip)
- [Delete] → `phx-click="delete_task"` with task_id → server dispatches DeleteTask command → dialog closes on success

### 9. Accessibility Notes

- All interactive elements have visible focus indicators (daisyUI provides this)
- Delete confirmation prevents accidental deletions
- Drag handle has `aria-grabbed`, `aria-dropeffect` attributes
- Keyboard support: Enter/Escape in inline edit, Enter in add form
- Screen reader: task list is `role="list"`, tasks are `role="listitem"`
- Color contrast meets WCAG AA in both light and dark themes (daisyUI defaults)
- Empty states are announced (not just visual)

### 10. daisyUI Component Usage

Map daisyUI components to our design:

| Our Component | daisyUI Base | Customization |
|---|---|---|
| Badge | `badge badge-primary` | Shows count |
| TaskInput | `input input-bordered` | With join for input+button group |
| AddButton | `btn btn-primary` | In join group |
| CompleteCheckbox | `checkbox checkbox-primary checkbox-sm` | Unchecked state |
| ReopenButton | `btn btn-ghost btn-sm` | Text only |
| DeleteButton | `btn btn-ghost btn-sm` | Error color on hover |
| ConfirmDeleteDialog | `modal` + `modal-box` | Standard modal pattern |
| ActiveTaskItem | `flex items-center gap-2` | Custom flex row |
| TaskPageHeader | `flex justify-between items-center` | Custom header |

Keep the document focused and practical. No fluff. This should be a working reference for implementation.

## Context

This is an event-sourced Phoenix/LiveView todo list app that uses:
- **Tailwind CSS + daisyUI** for styling
- **Heroicons** for icons
- **Phoenix LiveView streams** for efficient list rendering
- **PubSub** for real-time updates
- Light and dark theme support via `data-theme` attribute

The app has two views:
1. **Active Tasks** (`/`) — main view with add form, drag-reorderable task list
2. **Completed Tasks** (`/completed`) — list of done tasks with reopen/delete

## Existing Components (in `lib/todo_list_web/components/core_components.ex`)

These already exist and should be referenced, not recreated:
- `flash/1` — toast-style flash messages (info/error)
- `button/1` — button with navigation support, primary variant
- `input/1` — form inputs (text, select, textarea, checkbox) with error handling
- `header/1` — page header with title, subtitle, actions slots
- `icon/1` — Heroicons wrapper
- `show/2`, `hide/2` — JS transition helpers

Also existing in `lib/todo_list_web/components/layouts.ex`:
- `app/1` — app layout with navbar
- `flash_group/1` — flash message container
- `theme_toggle/1` — system/light/dark theme switcher

## Wireframes (from event model)

### Active Tasks View
```
+--------------------------------------------------+
|  My Tasks                        [Completed (3)]  |
+--------------------------------------------------+
|                                                    |
|  [ What needs to be done?            ] [+ Add]    |
|                                                    |
|  ::  Buy groceries                    [v] [ x ]   |
|  ::  Call the dentist                 [v] [ x ]   |
|  ::  Fix the leaky faucet            [v] [ x ]   |
|  ::  Write thank-you notes           [v] [ x ]   |
|                                                    |
+--------------------------------------------------+
```

Empty state:
```
|         No tasks yet. Add one above!               |
```

### Completed Tasks View
```
+--------------------------------------------------+
|  [< Back to Tasks]              Completed Tasks   |
+--------------------------------------------------+
|                                                    |
|  ~~Pick up dry cleaning~~       [Reopen]  [ x ]   |
|  ~~Send the invoice~~           [Reopen]  [ x ]   |
|  ~~Order new lightbulbs~~       [Reopen]  [ x ]   |
|                                                    |
+--------------------------------------------------+
```

### Inline Edit (ChangeTaskDescription)
```
Before:    ::  Call the dentist                 [v] [ x ]
Editing:   ::  [Call the dentist office____]    [v] [ x ]
After:     ::  Call the dentist office          [v] [ x ]
```

### Delete Confirmation Dialog
```
+--------------------------------------------+
|  Delete Task?                               |
|                                             |
|  "Fix the leaky faucet" will be             |
|  permanently removed.                       |
|                                             |
|           [Cancel]  [Delete]                |
+--------------------------------------------+
```

### Drag-and-Drop Reorder (ReprioritizeTask)
The `::` prefix is a drag handle. Users grab it to reorder tasks.

## Interactions Summary

| Interaction | Trigger | Component |
|---|---|---|
| Add task | Type + Enter or click [+ Add] | AddTaskForm |
| Complete task | Click checkbox [v] | ActiveTaskItem |
| Delete task | Click [x], confirm in dialog | DeleteButton + ConfirmDialog |
| Reopen task | Click [Reopen] | CompletedTaskItem |
| Edit description | Click description text → inline edit → Enter/blur | ActiveTaskItem |
| Reorder tasks | Drag handle (::) drop at new position | ActiveTaskList |
| Navigate to completed | Click [Completed (N)] badge | PageHeader |
| Navigate back to active | Click [< Back to Tasks] | PageHeader |

## Theme Configuration

CSS uses daisyUI themes with OKLch colors:
- **Dark theme**: Purple-tinted dark backgrounds, purple primary (`oklch(58% 0.233 277.117)`)
- **Light theme**: Clean white backgrounds, warm orange primary (`oklch(70% 0.213 47.604)`)
- Both themes share: `border-radius: 0.25rem` (fields), `0.5rem` (boxes), `1.5px` borders
