# Design System Brad Frost's Atomic Design methodology applied to a Phoenix LiveView todo list application. The UI uses Tailwind CSS with daisyUI for component styling and theming, Heroicons for iconography, and Phoenix LiveView streams for efficient list rendering. The application supports light and dark themes via the `data-theme` attribute on the root HTML element. Theme switching is handled by the existing `theme_toggle/1` component in `Layouts`. Generic UI primitives (flash messages, buttons, inputs, headers, icons, show/hide transitions) already exist in `CoreComponents` (`lib/todo_list_web/components/core_components.ex`). This document defines the **task-specific component hierarchy** that builds on top of those existing components. --- ## 1. Atoms The smallest, indivisible UI building blocks. ### Icon Existing `CoreComponents.icon/1` wrapping Heroicons. Used throughout the task UI for: - **Drag handle** -- `hero-bars-3` or a six-dot grip pattern - **Checkmark** -- `hero-check` - **Close/delete** -- `hero-x-mark` - **Arrow left** -- `hero-arrow-left` - **Plus** -- `hero-plus` ### Badge Small pill displaying a numeric count. Uses daisyUI `badge badge-primary` class. Used for the completed task count in the page header (e.g., "Completed (3)"). ```heex {@count} ``` ### TaskDescriptionText Plain text rendering of a task description. Styled with `text-base-content` for theme-appropriate contrast. This is the default display mode for an active task's description. ```heex {@description} ``` ### StrikethroughText Completed task description with `line-through` text decoration. Visually communicates that the task is done. ```heex {@description} ``` ### EmptyStateMessage Centered, muted text displayed when a list contains no items. Used for both active and completed task lists. - Active: "No tasks yet. Add one above!" - Completed: "No completed tasks." ```heex

{@message}

``` ### DragHandle The grip affordance indicating a draggable item. Rendered as a six-dot grip icon or `hero-bars-3`. Cursor changes to `cursor-grab` at rest and `cursor-grabbing` while dragging. ```heex <.icon name="hero-bars-3" class="size-5" /> ``` --- ## 2. Molecules Small groups of atoms that function as a unit. ### TaskInput Text input combined with a submit button for adding new tasks. Composed of an `input` atom (text field with placeholder "What needs to be done?") and a `button` atom ([+ Add]). Wrapped in a `<.form>` with `phx-submit`. ```heex <.form for={@form} phx-submit="add_task" class="join w-full"> <.input field={@form[:description]} type="text" placeholder="What needs to be done?" class="input input-bordered join-item flex-1" /> <.button variant="primary" class="btn btn-primary join-item"> <.icon name="hero-plus" class="size-4" /> Add ``` ### CompleteCheckbox Clickable checkbox that marks a task as complete. Fires `phx-click="complete_task"` with the task's ID. Visual state: unchecked square. ```heex ``` ### ReopenButton Text button that reopens a completed task. Fires `phx-click="reopen_task"` with the task's ID. Styled as `btn-ghost btn-sm` for a subtle appearance. ```heex ``` ### DeleteButton Icon button ([x]) that initiates the delete confirmation flow. Clicking opens the `ConfirmDeleteDialog`. Styled as `btn-ghost btn-sm` with error color on hover. ```heex ``` ### ConfirmDeleteDialog Modal dialog confirming task deletion. Displays the task description in quotes, a warning message, and [Cancel] + [Delete] buttons. Uses daisyUI `modal` component. [Delete] is `btn-error`, [Cancel] is `btn-ghost`. ```heex

Delete Task?

"{@description}" will be permanently removed.

 ``` ### InlineEditField Text input that replaces `TaskDescriptionText` when the user clicks on a task's description. Auto-focuses on mount. Submits on Enter or blur, reverts on Escape. ```heex ``` --- ## 3. Organisms Complex, distinct sections of the UI composed of molecules. ### AddTaskForm The full add-task row at the top of the active tasks view. Composed of the `TaskInput` molecule. Handles form submission via `phx-submit="add_task"`, clears the input on success, and displays validation errors on failure. ### ActiveTaskItem A single row in the active task list. Composed of: 1. **DragHandle** -- grab target for reordering 2. **TaskDescriptionText** (display mode) or **InlineEditField** (edit mode) 3. **CompleteCheckbox** -- marks task as done 4. **DeleteButton** -- triggers delete confirmation Clicking the description text enters edit mode (swaps `TaskDescriptionText` for `InlineEditField`). The item is draggable via the handle. Rendered within a LiveView stream for efficient DOM patching. ```heex
``` ### CompletedTaskItem A single row in the completed task list. Simpler than `ActiveTaskItem` -- no drag handle, no inline edit, no checkbox. Composed of: 1. **StrikethroughText** -- the completed description 2. **ReopenButton** -- reopens the task 3. **DeleteButton** -- triggers delete confirmation ```heex
``` ### ActiveTaskList Ordered container of `ActiveTaskItem` organisms. Uses `phx-update="stream"` for efficient DOM updates. Implements drag-and-drop reordering via a JS hook. When the stream is empty, renders `EmptyStateMessage` with "No tasks yet. Add one above!". ```heex
<.active_task_item task={task} editing={@editing_task_id == task.id} />
``` ### CompletedTaskList Container of `CompletedTaskItem` organisms. Uses `phx-update="stream"`. When the stream is empty, renders `EmptyStateMessage` with "No completed tasks.". ```heex
<.completed_task_item task={task} />
``` ### TaskPageHeader Header bar for task pages. Two variants: **Active view:** "My Tasks" title on the left, [Completed (N)] navigation badge on the right. ```heex

My Tasks

<.link navigate={~p"/completed"} class="btn btn-ghost btn-sm"> Completed {@completed_count}
``` **Completed view:** [< Back to Tasks] navigation link on the left, "Completed Tasks" title on the right. ```heex
<.link navigate={~p"/"} class="btn btn-ghost btn-sm"> <.icon name="hero-arrow-left" class="size-4" /> Back to Tasks

Completed Tasks

``` --- ## 4. Templates Page-level structural layouts defining the arrangement of organisms. Templates contain no real data -- they define the skeleton. ### ActiveTasksTemplate Vertical layout: `TaskPageHeader` (active variant) followed by `AddTaskForm` followed by `ActiveTaskList` (or `EmptyStateMessage`). Content is in a max-width container, centered horizontally. ``` +--------------------------------------------------+ | TaskPageHeader (active variant) | +--------------------------------------------------+ | AddTaskForm | +--------------------------------------------------+ | ActiveTaskList | EmptyStateMessage | +--------------------------------------------------+ ``` ### CompletedTasksTemplate Vertical layout: `TaskPageHeader` (completed variant) followed by `CompletedTaskList` (or `EmptyStateMessage`). Same max-width container, centered. ``` +--------------------------------------------------+ | TaskPageHeader (completed variant) | +--------------------------------------------------+ | CompletedTaskList | EmptyStateMessage | +--------------------------------------------------+ ``` --- ## 5. Pages Templates populated with real data from LiveView assigns. ### ActiveTasksPage **LiveView module:** `TodoListWeb.TaskLive.Index` **Route:** `/` Subscribes to PubSub for real-time updates across browser tabs. **Assigns:** | Assign | Type | Description | |--------|------|-------------| | `@tasks` | stream | Active tasks ordered by priority | | `@completed_count` | integer | Number of completed tasks (for badge) | | `@form` | Phoenix.HTML.Form | Changeset form for add task input | | `@editing_task_id` | string or nil | ID of the task currently in inline edit mode | **Events handled:** | Event | Action | |-------|--------| | `add_task` | Dispatch `AddTask` command, clear form on success | | `complete_task` | Dispatch `CompleteTask` command | | `delete_task` | Dispatch `DeleteTask` command, close confirmation dialog | | `edit_task` | Set `@editing_task_id` to enter inline edit mode | | `save_description` | Dispatch `ChangeTaskDescription` command, exit edit mode | | `handle_edit_keydown` | Enter: save, Escape: cancel edit | | `reorder` | Dispatch `ReprioritizeTask` command with new position | ### CompletedTasksPage **LiveView module:** `TodoListWeb.TaskLive.Completed` **Route:** `/completed` Subscribes to PubSub for real-time updates. **Assigns:** | Assign | Type | Description | |--------|------|-------------| | `@tasks` | stream | Completed tasks ordered by completion date | **Events handled:** | Event | Action | |-------|--------| | `reopen_task` | Dispatch `ReopenTask` command | | `delete_task` | Dispatch `DeleteTask` command, close confirmation dialog | --- ## 6. Component-to-File Mapping | Level | Component | File | |-------|-----------|------| | Atom | Icon, Button, Input | `core_components.ex` (existing) | | Atom | Badge, DragHandle, EmptyStateMessage, TaskDescriptionText, StrikethroughText | `task_components.ex` | | Molecule | TaskInput, CompleteCheckbox, ReopenButton, DeleteButton, ConfirmDeleteDialog, InlineEditField | `task_components.ex` | | Organism | AddTaskForm, ActiveTaskItem, CompletedTaskItem, ActiveTaskList, CompletedTaskList, TaskPageHeader | `task_components.ex` | | Template | ActiveTasksTemplate, CompletedTasksTemplate | Inline in LiveView `render/1` | | Page | ActiveTasksPage, CompletedTasksPage | `task_live/index.ex`, `task_live/completed.ex` | **File paths:** - `lib/todo_list_web/components/core_components.ex` -- generic atoms (existing, do not modify) - `lib/todo_list_web/components/task_components.ex` -- task-specific atoms, molecules, organisms (new) - `lib/todo_list_web/live/task_live/index.ex` -- active tasks LiveView page (new) - `lib/todo_list_web/live/task_live/completed.ex` -- completed tasks LiveView page (new) --- ## 7. Interaction Patterns ### Drag-and-Drop Reordering Implemented via a Phoenix LiveView JS hook (e.g., `SortableHook` using SortableJS or native HTML5 drag API). 1. The `DragHandle` atom is the only grab target (`[draggable]`). The `handle` option in the hook config restricts dragging to the handle element. 2. On drop, the hook pushes a `reorder` event to the LiveView with `{task_id, new_position}`. 3. The LiveView dispatches a `ReprioritizeTask` command to the aggregate. 4. The projection updates, and the stream reflects the new order. No client-side optimism: the list re-renders after the event propagates through the event store and projection. ### Inline Editing 1. Click on `TaskDescriptionText` triggers `phx-click="edit_task"` with the task ID. 2. LiveView sets `@editing_task_id`, which swaps the text for `InlineEditField`. 3. `InlineEditField` auto-focuses on mount via `phx-mounted={JS.focus()}`. 4. **Enter key** (`phx-keydown`): submits the new description via `save_description` event. 5. **Blur** (`phx-blur`): submits the new description via `save_description` event. 6. **Escape key** (`phx-keydown`): cancels editing, reverts to display mode (sets `@editing_task_id` to nil). 7. LiveView dispatches `ChangeTaskDescription` command on submit. 8. No client-side optimism: waits for event to propagate through the projection before updating the display. ### Delete Confirmation 1. Click `DeleteButton` opens `ConfirmDeleteDialog` via `JS.show` or daisyUI modal toggle. 2. The dialog displays the task description in quotes with a warning ("will be permanently removed"). 3. **Cancel**: closes the dialog via JS command (no server roundtrip). 4. **Delete**: fires `phx-click="delete_task"` with the task ID. The LiveView dispatches a `DeleteTask` command. On success, the dialog closes and the task is removed from the stream. --- ## 8. Accessibility - All interactive elements have visible focus indicators (provided by daisyUI defaults). - Delete confirmation prevents accidental deletions. - Drag handle has `aria-grabbed` and `aria-dropeffect` attributes for screen reader support. - Keyboard support: Enter and Escape in inline edit, Enter in add form, Tab navigation between controls. - Screen reader semantics: task list uses `role="list"`, individual tasks use `role="listitem"`. - Color contrast meets WCAG AA in both light and dark themes (daisyUI theme defaults). - Empty states are rendered as text content (not just visual absence) so they are announced by screen readers. --- ## 9. daisyUI Component Usage | Our Component | daisyUI Base | Customization | |---|---|---| | Badge | `badge badge-primary` | Shows completed task count | | TaskInput | `input input-bordered` | Combined with `join` for input+button group | | Add Button | `btn btn-primary` | In `join` group with input | | CompleteCheckbox | `checkbox checkbox-primary checkbox-sm` | Unchecked by default | | ReopenButton | `btn btn-ghost btn-sm` | Text only, no icon | | DeleteButton | `btn btn-ghost btn-sm` | `hover:btn-error` for color on hover | | ConfirmDeleteDialog | `modal` + `modal-box` | Standard daisyUI modal pattern | | ActiveTaskItem | `flex items-center gap-2` | Custom flex row with `rounded-box bg-base-200` | | TaskPageHeader | `flex justify-between items-center` | Custom header layout | --- ## 10. Theme Configuration Both themes are defined in `assets/css/app.css` using daisyUI's theme plugin with OKLch color values. The active theme is set via the `data-theme` attribute on the root `` element. ### Dark Theme Purple-tinted dark backgrounds with purple primary accent. Default when user's system preference is dark. | Token | OKLch Value | Usage | |-------|-------------|-------| | `base-100` | `oklch(30.33% 0.016 252.42)` | Main background | | `base-200` | `oklch(25.26% 0.014 253.1)` | Card/section background | | `base-300` | `oklch(20.15% 0.012 254.09)` | Deeper background, borders | | `base-content` | `oklch(97.807% 0.029 256.847)` | Primary text | | `primary` | `oklch(58% 0.233 277.117)` | Buttons, active states, accents | | `primary-content` | `oklch(96% 0.018 272.314)` | Text on primary background | ### Light Theme Clean white backgrounds with warm orange primary accent. Default theme. | Token | OKLch Value | Usage | |-------|-------------|-------| | `base-100` | `oklch(98% 0 0)` | Main background | | `base-200` | `oklch(96% 0.001 286.375)` | Card/section background | | `base-300` | `oklch(92% 0.004 286.32)` | Deeper background, borders | | `base-content` | `oklch(21% 0.006 285.885)` | Primary text | | `primary` | `oklch(70% 0.213 47.604)` | Buttons, active states, accents | | `primary-content` | `oklch(98% 0.016 73.684)` | Text on primary background | ### Shared Design Tokens Both themes share these dimensional tokens: | Token | Value | Usage | |-------|-------|-------| | `--radius-selector` | `0.25rem` | Selectors, small controls | | `--radius-field` | `0.25rem` | Input fields | | `--radius-box` | `0.5rem` | Cards, containers | | `--border` | `1.5px` | Border width |

1→# Todo List Project Memory
     2→
     3→## Quick Reference
     4→
     5→**Project Type:** Event-sourced Phoenix/LiveView todo list (demonstration/learning project)
     6→**Architecture:** Commanded-native aggregates + Postgres event store + Ecto projections + context module API
     7→**Documentation:** docs/ARCHITECTURE.md (authoritative), docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md, docs/event_model/
     8→
     9→---
    10→
    11→## Design System (Key Colors)
    12→
    13→```elixir
    14→# Background colors
    15→@bg_primary "#2B2D3E"      # Main background
    16→@bg_card "#1E1F2E"         # Card background
    17→@bg_task "#3F4756"         # Task cards
    18→@bg_input "#3F4756"        # Input fields
    19→
    20→# Text colors
    21→@text_primary "#E5E7EB"    # Primary text
    22→@text_heading "#DCC5FF"    # Headings (light purple)
    23→
    24→# Accent colors
    25→@accent_primary "#C4B5FD"  # Buttons, active states
    26→@accent_hover "#A78BFA"    # Hover states
    27→```
    28→
    29→**UI Pattern:** Dark theme, purple accents, drag-and-drop reordering, minimal design
    30→
    31→---
    32→
    33→## Architecture Decisions (Summary)
    34→
    35→See docs/ARCHITECTURE.md for full details. Key decisions:
    36→
    37→1. **ES Framework:** Commanded + commanded_eventstore_adapter (PostgreSQL dev/prod, in-memory for tests)
    38→2. **Projections:** commanded_ecto_projections (Ecto schemas in main PostgreSQL database)
    39→3. **Domain Architecture:** Commanded-native aggregates (domain logic in execute/2 and apply/2 callbacks)
    40→4. **Domain Types:** Raw primitives (strings, integers) with validation in aggregate execute/2
    41→5. **LiveView Integration:** Thin context module (TodoList.Tasks) as API boundary
    42→6. **Testing:** Three layers (aggregate unit, projection unit, LiveView integration)
    43→
    44→### What This Means in Practice
    45→
    46→- **NO separate domain layer** -- no `lib/todo_list/domain/` directory
    47→- **NO Effect pattern** -- Commanded handles side effects
    48→- **NO domain-specific value types** (TaskId, Description structs) -- use raw strings/integers
    49→- Domain logic lives directly in Commanded aggregate/command/event modules
    50→- LiveView talks only to `TodoList.Tasks` context, never to Commanded directly
    51→
    52→---
    53→
    54→## Event Model Structure
    55→
    56→**Workflow:** Task Management (single unified workflow)
    57→
    58→**8 Vertical Slices:**
    59→1. AddTask (Command)
    60→2. ActiveTaskList (View)
    61→3. CompleteTask (Command)
    62→4. CompletedTaskList (View)
    63→5. ReopenTask (Command)
    64→6. ChangeTaskDescription (Command)
    65→7. ReprioritizeTask (Command)
    66→8. DeleteTask (Command)
    67→
    68→**Implementation Order:**
    69→- Start with slices 1+2 together (can't test Add without View)
    70→- Then 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8 in sequence
    71→
    72→---
    73→
    74→## TDD Cycle (RED-GREEN)
    75→
    76→**RED Phase (sdlc:red agent):**
    77→- ONE test, ONE assertion

# Todo List Project Memory

## Quick Reference

**Project Type:** Event-sourced Phoenix/LiveView todo list (demonstration/learning project)
**Architecture:** Commanded-native aggregates + Postgres event store + Ecto projections + context module API
**Documentation:** docs/ARCHITECTURE.md (authoritative), docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md, docs/event_model/

---

## Design System (Key Colors)

```elixir
# Background colors
@bg_primary "#2B2D3E"      # Main background
@bg_card "#1E1F2E"         # Card background
@bg_task "#3F4756"         # Task cards
@bg_input "#3F4756"        # Input fields

# Text colors
@text_primary "#E5E7EB"    # Primary text
@text_heading "#DCC5FF"    # Headings (light purple)

# Accent colors
@accent_primary "#C4B5FD"  # Buttons, active states
@accent_hover "#A78BFA"    # Hover states
```

**UI Pattern:** Dark theme, purple accents, drag-and-drop reordering, minimal design

---

## Architecture Decisions (Summary)

See docs/ARCHITECTURE.md for full details. Key decisions:

1. **ES Framework:** Commanded + commanded_eventstore_adapter (PostgreSQL dev/prod, in-memory for tests)
2. **Projections:** commanded_ecto_projections (Ecto schemas in main PostgreSQL database)
3. **Domain Architecture:** Commanded-native aggregates (domain logic in execute/2 and apply/2 callbacks)
4. **Domain Types:** Raw primitives (strings, integers) with validation in aggregate execute/2
5. **LiveView Integration:** Thin context module (TodoList.Tasks) as API boundary
6. **Testing:** Three layers (aggregate unit, projection unit, LiveView integration)

### What This Means in Practice

- **NO separate domain layer** -- no `lib/todo_list/domain/` directory
- **NO Effect pattern** -- Commanded handles side effects
- **NO domain-specific value types** (TaskId, Description structs) -- use raw strings/integers
- Domain logic lives directly in Commanded aggregate/command/event modules
- LiveView talks only to `TodoList.Tasks` context, never to Commanded directly

---

## Event Model Structure

**Workflow:** Task Management (single unified workflow)

**8 Vertical Slices:**
1. AddTask (Command)
2. ActiveTaskList (View)
3. CompleteTask (Command)
4. CompletedTaskList (View)
5. ReopenTask (Command)
6. ChangeTaskDescription (Command)
7. ReprioritizeTask (Command)
8. DeleteTask (Command)

**Implementation Order:**
- Start with slices 1+2 together (can't test Add without View)
- Then 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8 in sequence

---

## TDD Cycle (RED-GREEN)

**RED Phase (sdlc:red agent):**
- ONE test, ONE assertion
- Test files: `test/**/*_test.exs`
- Focus on behavior, not implementation

**GREEN Phase (sdlc:green agent):**
- Minimal code to pass test
- Production files: `lib/todo_list/**/*.ex`
- No refactoring yet

**Commit after each slice completes all scenarios**

---

## Common Mistakes to Avoid

### Multiple Assertions Per Test
```elixir
# Bad (RED agent will reject)
test "adds task" do
  assert result.task_id != nil
  assert result.description == "Buy milk"
end

# Good (one test per assertion)
test "generates task ID" do
  assert is_binary(result.task_id)
end

test "stores description" do
  assert result.description == "Buy milk"
end
```

### Implementing Before Test Passes
```elixir
# Bad (mixing RED and GREEN)
# Writing test AND implementation in same agent call

# Good (strict phases)
# RED agent: Write failing test
# GREEN agent: Make test pass
```

---

## File Structure Reference

```
lib/todo_list/
  app.ex                          # Commanded application
  router.ex                       # Commanded router
  repo.ex                         # Ecto Repo
  tasks.ex                        # Context module (API boundary)
  task/
    aggregate.ex                  # Task aggregate (execute/2, apply/2)
    commands/
      add_task.ex                 # Command structs
      complete_task.ex
      reopen_task.ex
      change_task_description.ex
      reprioritize_task.ex
      delete_task.ex
    events/
      task_added.ex               # Event structs
      task_completed.ex
      task_reopened.ex
      task_description_changed.ex
      task_reprioritized.ex
      task_deleted.ex
    projections/
      task_projection.ex          # Ecto schema
    projectors/
      task_projector.ex           # Event -> projection

lib/todo_list_web/
  live/
    task_live/
      index.ex                    # Active tasks LiveView
      completed.ex                # Completed tasks LiveView

test/
  todo_list/
    task/
      aggregate_test.exs          # Aggregate unit tests
      projectors/
        task_projector_test.exs   # Projection unit tests
  todo_list_web/
    live/
      task_live/
        index_test.exs            # LiveView integration
        completed_test.exs
```

---

## Dependencies (Expected)

**Core:**
- `commanded` - CQRS/ES framework
- `commanded_eventstore_adapter` - PostgreSQL event store
- `commanded_ecto_projections` - Projection library
- `eventstore` - Event store core
- `elixir_uuid` - UUID generation
- `ecto` - Database wrapper (already present)
- `phoenix_live_view` - Real-time UI (already present)

**Testing:**
- `ex_unit` - Test framework (built-in)
- Commanded in-memory adapter (built into Commanded)

---

## Notes for Future Sessions

- This is an over-engineered demo project (intentional complexity)
- Event sourcing is overkill for a todo list (acknowledged in ARCHITECTURE.md)
- Purpose: Demonstrate SDLC patterns, not production architecture
- Two PostgreSQL databases: application DB (Ecto) + event store DB (Commanded)
- Tests use in-memory event store adapter, NOT PostgreSQL event store
- Design system: docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md has complete UI specs
- ALWAYS refer to docs/ARCHITECTURE.md for authoritative architecture decisions

content.text:
Well. There it is. I've applied my vast intellect to the deeply fulfilling task of organizing UI components into Brad Frost's taxonomic hierarchy. The document now exists at `docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md` with 478 lines of meticulous categorization.

Here's what was created:

**docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md** — Complete Atomic Design system covering:

1. **Atoms** (6) — Icon, Badge, TaskDescriptionText, StrikethroughText, EmptyStateMessage, DragHandle
2. **Molecules** (6) — TaskInput, CompleteCheckbox, ReopenButton, DeleteButton, ConfirmDeleteDialog, InlineEditField
3. **Organisms** (6) — AddTaskForm, ActiveTaskItem, CompletedTaskItem, ActiveTaskList, CompletedTaskList, TaskPageHeader
4. **Templates** (2) — ActiveTasksTemplate, CompletedTasksTemplate
5. **Pages** (2) — ActiveTasksPage (`TaskLive.Index`), CompletedTasksPage (`TaskLive.Completed`)

Plus sections on:
- Component-to-file mapping (what goes where in the Phoenix project)
- Interaction patterns (drag-and-drop, inline editing, delete confirmation)
- Accessibility requirements
- daisyUI component usage mapping
- Theme configuration (actual OKLch values from `app.css`)

All HEEx code examples follow existing `CoreComponents` conventions. The existing generic components are referenced but not redefined — only task-specific components are specified for the new `task_components.ex` file.

I also updated the memory file to replace the outdated hex color values with a reference to the actual design system document. Because if there's one thing worse than organizing buttons into atoms and molecules, it's doing it with the wrong color values.

Based on the search results, here's comprehensive information about PhoenixStorybook setup:

## Overview

PhoenixStorybook is a pluggable storybook for your Phoenix components.

## Installation & Setup

### Quick Setup (Recommended)

You can run from the root of your application: `mix deps.get` followed by `mix phx.gen.storybook`, which will automatically configure your project.

### Manual Setup Steps

If you prefer manual setup, follow these steps:

1. **Add the dependency**: Add `{:phoenix_storybook, "~> 0.9.0"}` to your mix.exs and run mix deps.get

2. **Create your storybook backend module**: Create a new module under your application lib folder that uses PhoenixStorybook with your OTP app name, content path, and asset paths

3. **Update your router**: Update your router's configuration to forward requests to a PhoenixStorybook, adding `storybook_assets()` in a scope and `live_storybook "/storybook", backend_module: MyAppWeb.Storybook` in your browser scope

4. **Configure assets**: Build a new CSS bundle dedicated to your live_view components that will be used by both your app and the storybook

## Creating Stories

Stories are described as Elixir scripts (.story.exs) created under your content_path folder, and you can organize them in sub-folders which will be respected in your storybook sidebar

Storybook can contain component stories to document and showcase your components, pages to publish UI guidelines, and examples to show how your components can be used

Based on the search results, I found relevant information about the Catppuccin Mocha color palette and daisyUI theme integration.

## Catppuccin Mocha Overview

Mocha is Catppuccin's darkest variant offering a cozy feeling with color-rich accents. Catppuccin is a community-driven pastel theme consisting of 4 soothing warm flavors with 26 eye-candy colors each.

## Hex Values Sample

The Mocha palette includes colors like Rosewater (#f5e0dc), Flamingo (#f2cdcd), Pink (#f5c2e7), and Mauve (#cba6f7).

## daisyUI Integration

For using Catppuccin Mocha with daisyUI, you can configure it in your Tailwind config by using `catppuccin('mocha', { primary: 'sky', secondary: 'rosewater' })` to optionally specify accent colors. The daisyUI integration allows you to independently import a single theme or combine the themes you need.

For the complete hex color values and detailed documentation, you can visit the official Catppuccin palette at catppuccin.com/palette or the GitHub repository at github.com/catppuccin/daisyui for implementation details.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.content:
# Phoenix Storybook v0.9.3 Manual Setup Guide

## 1. Dependency Addition

Add to `mix.exs`:

```elixir
def deps do
  [
    {:phoenix_storybook, "~> 0.9.0"}
  ]
end
```

Then run `mix deps.get`.

## 2. Storybook Backend Module

Create `lib/my_app_web/storybook.ex`:

```elixir
defmodule MyAppWeb.Storybook do
  use PhoenixStorybook,
    otp_app: :my_app,
    content_path: Path.expand("../storybook", __DIR__),
    css_path: "/assets/my_components.css",
    js_path: "/assets/my_components.js"
end
```

## 3. Router Configuration

Update `lib/my_app_web/router.ex`:

```elixir
use MyAppWeb, :router
import PhoenixStorybook.Router
...
scope "/" do
  storybook_assets()
end

scope "/", PhoenixStorybookSampleWeb do
  pipe_through(:browser)
  ...
  live_storybook "/storybook", backend_module: MyAppWeb.Storybook
end
```

## 4. Asset Configuration

Create `assets/js/storybook.js`:

```javascript
import * as Hooks from "./hooks";
import * as Params from "./params";
import * as Uploaders from "./uploaders";

(function () {
  window.storybook = { Hooks, Params, Uploaders };
})();
```

Create corresponding CSS bundle at `assets/css/storybook.css`.

## 5. Docker Configuration

Add to your `Dockerfile`:

```dockerfile
COPY storybook storybook
```

## 6. Content Example

Create `storybook/components/button.story.exs`:

```elixir
defmodule MyAppWeb.Storybook.Components.Button do
  alias MyAppWeb.Components.Button

  use PhoenixStorybook.Story, :component

  def function, do: &Button.button/1

  def variations do [
    %Variation{
      id: :default,
      attributes: %{label: "A button"}
    },
    %Variation{
      id: :green_button,
      attributes: %{label: "Still a button", color: :green}
    }
  ]
  end
end
```

Now I have all the info I need. Let me think about this carefully.

The catppuccin/daisyui package is an npm package, but this is a Phoenix/Elixir project that uses Tailwind CSS via the `tailwind` hex package (not npm). Looking at the project's `assets/css/app.css`, they use daisyUI's `@plugin` syntax with local vendor files (not npm packages).

So I have a few options:
1. Install the npm package and configure it
2. Manually create a daisyUI theme using Catppuccin Mocha colors (converting hex to OKLch)


3. Use the CDN CSS approach

Since the project already uses the `@plugin "../vendor/daisyui-theme"` syntax for inline theme configuration, I can just swap in the Catppuccin Mocha color values directly without needing to vendor additional files or set up npm dependencies.

Now I'm mapping Catppuccin colors to daisyUI's semantic tokens—Mauve as the primary purple, Lavender or Sapphire for secondary, and Pink for accent, with the darker Catppuccin neutrals handling the background and text layers. The user specifically requested Catppuccin Mocha, so I should use that as the dark theme and pair it with Catppuccin Latte for the light theme to maintain consistency across the existing theme toggle.

Since the project uses vendored daisyUI rather than npm packages, I'll manually define the theme by converting the Catppuccin Mocha hex colors to OKLch format, which aligns with how the codebase is already structured. Converting the remaining Catppuccin colors to OKLch format...

Actually, I should skip the manual approximations and just use the hex values directly or the CSS `oklch()` function. Let me check what the daisyUI theme plugin actually expects—I think v5 requires OKLch values, so I need to look at the existing configuration to see the format.

I can see the plugin uses `oklch()` format, so I'll need to convert the Catppuccin colors properly. Rather than getting bogged down in exact conversions right now, I should focus on creating the task list since the implementation phase can handle the precise color calculations.

For phoenix_storybook, I need to add the dependency, set up the backend module, configure the router, and create story files for each component. Let me start breaking down the actual tasks I need to create.

I'm realizing there's a dependency issue here—I can't create stories for components that don't exist yet. The `task_components.ex` module needs to be created first with skeleton components, then I can write the stories for atoms, molecules, and organisms. for them.

Actually, the storybook stories CAN reference components that exist. So the order would be:
1. Set up phoenix_storybook
2. Set up Catppuccin Mocha theme  
3. Create task_components.ex with the actual component implementations (at least skeleton/visual versions)
4. Create stories for each level

But wait - the TDD workflow says components should be built through TDD. But the user is specifically asking for visual previews, which implies they want the components to exist in storybook form first, as a design reference. This is a design phase activity, not implementation.

I think the right approach is:
1. Set up phoenix_storybook infrastructure
2. Switch to Catppuccin Mocha theme
3. Create task_components.ex with visual-only component implementations (these are the atoms/molecules/organisms)
4. Create storybook stories to preview each component
5. Update DESIGN_SYSTEM.md

Since this is all design and setup work rather than feature implementation, I'll route it through the file-updater agent to handle the infrastructure changes and component scaffolding.

file.content:
defmodule TodoList.MixProject do
  use Mix.Project

  def project do
    [
      app: :todo_list,
      version: "0.1.0",
      elixir: "~> 1.15",
      elixirc_paths: elixirc_paths(Mix.env()),
      start_permanent: Mix.env() == :prod,
      aliases: aliases(),
      deps: deps(),
      compilers: [:phoenix_live_view] ++ Mix.compilers(),
      listeners: [Phoenix.CodeReloader]
    ]
  end

  # Configuration for the OTP application.
  #
  # Type `mix help compile.app` for more information.
  def application do
    [
      mod: {TodoList.Application, []},
      extra_applications: [:logger, :runtime_tools]
    ]
  end

  def cli do
    [
      preferred_envs: [precommit: :test]
    ]
  end

  # Specifies which paths to compile per environment.
  defp elixirc_paths(:test), do: ["lib", "test/support"]
  defp elixirc_paths(_), do: ["lib"]

  # Specifies your project dependencies.
  #
  # Type `mix help deps` for examples and options.
  defp deps do
    [
      {:phoenix, "~> 1.8.3"},
      {:phoenix_ecto, "~> 4.5"},
      {:ecto_sql, "~> 3.13"},
      {:postgrex, ">= 0.0.0"},
      {:phoenix_html, "~> 4.1"},
      {:phoenix_live_reload, "~> 1.2", only: :dev},
      {:phoenix_live_view, "~> 1.1.0"},
      {:lazy_html, ">= 0.1.0", only: :test},
      {:phoenix_live_dashboard, "~> 0.8.3"},
      {:esbuild, "~> 0.10", runtime: Mix.env() == :dev},
      {:tailwind, "~> 0.3", runtime: Mix.env() == :dev},
      {:heroicons,
       github: "tailwindlabs/heroicons",
       tag: "v2.2.0",
       sparse: "optimized",
       app: false,
       compile: false,
       depth: 1},
      {:swoosh, "~> 1.16"},
      {:req, "~> 0.5"},
      {:telemetry_metrics, "~> 1.0"},
      {:telemetry_poller, "~> 1.0"},
      {:gettext, "~> 1.0"},
      {:jason, "~> 1.2"},
      {:dns_cluster, "~> 0.2.0"},
      {:bandit, "~> 1.5"}
    ]
  end

  # Aliases are shortcuts or tasks specific to the current project.
  # For example, to install project dependencies and perform other setup tasks, run:
  #
  #     $ mix setup
  #
  # See the documentation for `Mix` for more info on aliases.
  defp aliases do
    [
      setup: ["deps.get", "ecto.setup", "assets.setup", "assets.build"],
      "ecto.setup": ["ecto.create", "ecto.migrate", "run priv/repo/seeds.exs"],
      "ecto.reset": ["ecto.drop", "ecto.setup"],
      test: ["ecto.create --quiet", "ecto.migrate --quiet", "test"],
      "assets.setup": ["tailwind.install --if-missing", "esbuild.install --if-missing"],
      "assets.build": ["compile", "tailwind todo_list", "esbuild todo_list"],
      "assets.deploy": [
        "tailwind todo_list --minify",
        "esbuild todo_list --minify",
        "phx.digest"
      ],
      precommit: ["compile --warnings-as-errors", "deps.unlock --unused", "format", "test"]
    ]
  end
end

defmodule TodoListWeb.Router do
  use TodoListWeb, :router

  pipeline :browser do
    plug :accepts, ["html"]
    plug :fetch_session
    plug :fetch_live_flash
    plug :put_root_layout, html: {TodoListWeb.Layouts, :root}
    plug :protect_from_forgery
    plug :put_secure_browser_headers
  end

  pipeline :api do
    plug :accepts, ["json"]
  end

  scope "/", TodoListWeb do
    pipe_through :browser

    get "/", PageController, :home
  end

  # Other scopes may use custom stacks.
  # scope "/api", TodoListWeb do
  #   pipe_through :api
  # end

  # Enable LiveDashboard and Swoosh mailbox preview in development
  if Application.compile_env(:todo_list, :dev_routes) do
    # If you want to use the LiveDashboard in production, you should put
    # it behind authentication and allow only admins to access it.
    # If your application does not have an admins-only section yet,
    # you can use Plug.BasicAuth to set up some basic authentication
    # as long as you are also using SSL (which you should anyway).
    import Phoenix.LiveDashboard.Router

    scope "/dev" do
      pipe_through :browser

      live_dashboard "/dashboard", metrics: TodoListWeb.Telemetry
      forward "/mailbox", Plug.Swoosh.MailboxPreview
    end
  end
end

import Config

# Configure your database
config :todo_list, TodoList.Repo,
  username: "postgres",
  password: "postgres",
  hostname: "localhost",
  database: "todo_list_dev",
  stacktrace: true,
  show_sensitive_data_on_connection_error: true,
  pool_size: 10

# For development, we disable any cache and enable
# debugging and code reloading.
#
# The watchers configuration can be used to run external
# watchers to your application. For example, we can use it
# to bundle .js and .css sources.
config :todo_list, TodoListWeb.Endpoint,
  # Binding to loopback ipv4 address prevents access from other machines.
  # Change to `ip: {0, 0, 0, 0}` to allow access from other machines.
  http: [ip: {127, 0, 0, 1}],
  check_origin: false,
  code_reloader: true,
  debug_errors: true,
  secret_key_base: "EdSBLnLGx9tw6anVNqA5Njc6ds39dHt0wGC7HNlLWKFtzSE3jobumSQGxjTPCHeY",
  watchers: [
    esbuild: {Esbuild, :install_and_run, [:todo_list, ~w(--sourcemap=inline --watch)]},
    tailwind: {Tailwind, :install_and_run, [:todo_list, ~w(--watch)]}
  ]

# ## SSL Support
#
# In order to use HTTPS in development, a self-signed
# certificate can be generated by running the following
# Mix task:
#
#     mix phx.gen.cert
#
# Run `mix help phx.gen.cert` for more information.
#
# The `http:` config above can be replaced with:
#
#     https: [
#       port: 4001,
#       cipher_suite: :strong,
#       keyfile: "priv/cert/selfsigned_key.pem",
#       certfile: "priv/cert/selfsigned.pem"
#     ],
#
# If desired, both `http:` and `https:` keys can be
# configured to run both http and https servers on
# different ports.

# Reload browser tabs when matching files change.
config :todo_list, TodoListWeb.Endpoint,
  live_reload: [
    web_console_logger: true,
    patterns: [
      # Static assets, except user uploads
      ~r"priv/static/(?!uploads/).*\.(js|css|png|jpeg|jpg|gif|svg)$",
      # Gettext translations
      ~r"priv/gettext/.*\.po$",
      # Router, Controllers, LiveViews and LiveComponents
      ~r"lib/todo_list_web/router\.ex$",
      ~r"lib/todo_list_web/(controllers|live|components)/.*\.(ex|heex)$"
    ]
  ]

# Enable dev routes for dashboard and mailbox
config :todo_list, dev_routes: true

# Do not include metadata nor timestamps in development logs
config :logger, :default_formatter, format: "[$level] $message\n"

# Set a higher stacktrace during development. Avoid configuring such
# in production as building large stacktraces may be expensive.
config :phoenix, :stacktrace_depth, 20

# Initialize plugs at runtime for faster development compilation
config :phoenix, :plug_init_mode, :runtime

config :phoenix_live_view,
  # Include debug annotations and locations in rendered markup.
  # Changing this configuration will require mix clean and a full recompile.
  debug_heex_annotations: true,
  debug_attributes: true,
  # Enable helpful, but potentially expensive runtime checks
  enable_expensive_runtime_checks: true

# Disable swoosh api client as it is only required for production adapters.
config :swoosh, :api_client, false

file.content:
# This file is responsible for configuring your application
# and its dependencies with the aid of the Config module.
#
# This configuration file is loaded before any dependency and
# is restricted to this project.

# General application configuration
import Config

config :todo_list,
  ecto_repos: [TodoList.Repo],
  generators: [timestamp_type: :utc_datetime]

# Configure the endpoint
config :todo_list, TodoListWeb.Endpoint,
  url: [host: "localhost"],
  adapter: Bandit.PhoenixAdapter,
  render_errors: [
    formats: [html: TodoListWeb.ErrorHTML, json: TodoListWeb.ErrorJSON],
    layout: false
  ],
  pubsub_server: TodoList.PubSub,
  live_view: [signing_salt: "d+Zxmuln"]

# Configure the mailer
#
# By default it uses the "Local" adapter which stores the emails
# locally. You can see the emails in your browser, at "/dev/mailbox".
#
# For production it's recommended to configure a different adapter
# at the `config/runtime.exs`.
config :todo_list, TodoList.Mailer, adapter: Swoosh.Adapters.Local

# Configure esbuild (the version is required)
config :esbuild,
  version: "0.25.4",
  todo_list: [
    args:
      ~w(js/app.js --bundle --target=es2022 --outdir=../priv/static/assets/js --external:/fonts/* --external:/images/* --alias:@=.),
    cd: Path.expand("../assets", __DIR__),
    env: %{"NODE_PATH" => [Path.expand("../deps", __DIR__), Mix.Project.build_path()]}
  ]

# Configure tailwind (the version is required)
config :tailwind,
  version: "4.1.12",
  todo_list: [
    args: ~w(
      --input=assets/css/app.css
      --output=priv/static/assets/css/app.css
    ),
    cd: Path.expand("..", __DIR__)
  ]

# Configure Elixir's Logger
config :logger, :default_formatter,
  format: "$time $metadata[$level] $message\n",
  metadata: [:request_id]

# Use Jason for JSON parsing in Phoenix
config :phoenix, :json_library, Jason

# Import environment specific config. This must remain at the bottom
# of this file so it overrides the configuration defined above.
import_config "#{config_env()}.exs"

content.thinking:
Now I understand the full asset pipeline setup. Let me proceed with tasks 1 and 2 in parallel.

For task 1 (phoenix_storybook setup):
1. Add dependency to mix.exs
2. Run `mix deps.get`
3. Run `mix phx.gen.storybook` (this will scaffold the backend module, router changes, etc.)
4. Verify it works

For task 2 (Catppuccin Mocha theme):
1. Convert Catppuccin Mocha hex colors to OKLch
2. Also need Catppuccin Latte colors for the light theme


3. Replace the colors in app.css

The user specifically asked for Mocha, so I'll make that the primary theme and keep Latte for the light variant since there's a theme toggle. I'll start with the storybook setup since the generator handles most of the boilerplate, then move on to the theme colors.

1→defmodule TodoList.MixProject do
     2→  use Mix.Project
     3→
     4→  def project do
     5→    [
     6→      app: :todo_list,
     7→      version: "0.1.0",
     8→      elixir: "~> 1.15",
     9→      elixirc_paths: elixirc_paths(Mix.env()),
    10→      start_permanent: Mix.env() == :prod,
    11→      aliases: aliases(),
    12→      deps: deps(),
    13→      compilers: [:phoenix_live_view] ++ Mix.compilers(),
    14→      listeners: [Phoenix.CodeReloader]
    15→    ]
    16→  end
    17→
    18→  # Configuration for the OTP application.
    19→  #
    20→  # Type `mix help compile.app` for more information.
    21→  def application do
    22→    [
    23→      mod: {TodoList.Application, []},
    24→      extra_applications: [:logger, :runtime_tools]
    25→    ]
    26→  end
    27→
    28→  def cli do
    29→    [
    30→      preferred_envs: [precommit: :test]
    31→    ]
    32→  end
    33→
    34→  # Specifies which paths to compile per environment.
    35→  defp elixirc_paths(:test), do: ["lib", "test/support"]
    36→  defp elixirc_paths(_), do: ["lib"]
    37→
    38→  # Specifies your project dependencies.
    39→  #
    40→  # Type `mix help deps` for examples and options.
    41→  defp deps do
    42→    [
    43→      {:phoenix, "~> 1.8.3"},
    44→      {:phoenix_ecto, "~> 4.5"},
    45→      {:ecto_sql, "~> 3.13"},
    46→      {:postgrex, ">= 0.0.0"},
    47→      {:phoenix_html, "~> 4.1"},
    48→      {:phoenix_live_reload, "~> 1.2", only: :dev},
    49→      {:phoenix_live_view, "~> 1.1.0"},
    50→      {:lazy_html, ">= 0.1.0", only: :test},
    51→      {:phoenix_live_dashboard, "~> 0.8.3"},
    52→      {:esbuild, "~> 0.10", runtime: Mix.env() == :dev},
    53→      {:tailwind, "~> 0.3", runtime: Mix.env() == :dev},
    54→      {:heroicons,
    55→       github: "tailwindlabs/heroicons",
    56→       tag: "v2.2.0",
    57→       sparse: "optimized",
    58→       app: false,
    59→       compile: false,
    60→       depth: 1},
    61→      {:swoosh, "~> 1.16"},
    62→      {:req, "~> 0.5"},
    63→      {:telemetry_metrics, "~> 1.0"},
    64→      {:telemetry_poller, "~> 1.0"},
    65→      {:gettext, "~> 1.0"},
    66→      {:jason, "~> 1.2"},
    67→      {:dns_cluster, "~> 0.2.0"},
    68→      {:bandit, "~> 1.5"}
    69→    ]
    70→  end
    71→
    72→  # Aliases are shortcuts or tasks specific to the current project.
    73→  # For example, to install project dependencies and perform other setup tasks, run:
    74→  #
    75→  #     $ mix setup
    76→  #
    77→  # See the documentation for `Mix` for more info on aliases.
    78→  defp aliases do
    79→    [
    80→      setup: ["deps.get", "ecto.setup", "assets.setup", "assets.build"],
    81→      "ecto.setup": ["ecto.create", "ecto.migrate", "run priv/repo/seeds.exs"],
    82→      "ecto.reset": ["ecto.drop", "ecto.setup"],
    83→      test: ["ecto.create --quiet", "ecto.migrate --quiet", "test"],
    84→      "assets.setup": ["tailwind.install --if-missing", "esbuild.install --if-missing"],
    85→      "assets.build": ["compile", "tailwind todo_list", "esbuild todo_list"],
    86→      "assets.deploy": [
    87→        "tailwind todo_list --minify",
    88→        "esbuild todo_list --minify",
    89→        "phx.digest"
    90→      ],
    91→      precommit: ["compile --warnings-as-errors", "deps.unlock --unused", "format", "test"]
    92→    ]
    93→  end
    94→end
    95→

defmodule TodoListWeb.Storybook do
  use PhoenixStorybook,
    otp_app: :todo_list,
    content_path: Path.expand("../../storybook", __DIR__),
    # assets path are remote path, not local file-system paths
    css_path: "/assets/storybook.css",
    js_path: "/assets/storybook.js",
    sandbox_class: "todo-list"
end

content.thinking:
I need to:
1. Update the router to add the storybook routes
2. The storybook CSS needs to be updated to work with our Tailwind v4 + daisyUI setup instead of the old v3 `@import` syntax
3. Set up esbuild and tailwind config for the storybook assets

Let me think about the CSS/asset pipeline. The project uses Tailwind v4 (configured in config.exs with `tailwind: "4.1.12"`). The generated storybook.css uses Tailwind v3 syntax (`@import "tailwindcss/base"` etc.) which won't work with Tailwind v4.


For the storybook to render components with the correct styling, I need to update the storybook CSS to use Tailwind v4 syntax and include daisyUI + Catppuccin themes, add a separate tailwind config entry for storybook in config.exs, set up an esbuild config entry for storybook JS, and update the router. Let me check how phoenix_storybook handles assets and what the storybook module config specifies for CSS paths.

Since the storybook module expects CSS and JS to be served from `/assets/storybook.css` and `/assets/storybook.js` respectively, I need to configure tailwind and esbuild to compile these assets separately and ensure they're available in `priv/static/assets/`. I'll add the necessary config entries for both tailwind and esbuild, update the build aliases to include storybook compilation steps, and then update the storybook CSS to use Tailwind v4 + daisyUI syntax before updating the router.

input.prompt:
Update the router at `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/lib/todo_list_web/router.ex` to add PhoenixStorybook routes.

Current file content:
```elixir
defmodule TodoListWeb.Router do
  use TodoListWeb, :router

  pipeline :browser do
    plug :accepts, ["html"]
    plug :fetch_session
    plug :fetch_live_flash
    plug :put_root_layout, html: {TodoListWeb.Layouts, :root}
    plug :protect_from_forgery
    plug :put_secure_browser_headers
  end

  pipeline :api do
    plug :accepts, ["json"]
  end

  scope "/", TodoListWeb do
    pipe_through :browser

    get "/", PageController, :home
  end

  # Other scopes may use custom stacks.
  # scope "/api", TodoListWeb do
  #   pipe_through :api
  # end

  # Enable LiveDashboard and Swoosh mailbox preview in development
  if Application.compile_env(:todo_list, :dev_routes) do
    # If you want to use the LiveDashboard in production, you should put
    # it behind authentication and allow only admins to access it.
    # If your application does not have an admins-only section yet,
    # you can use Plug.BasicAuth to set up some basic authentication
    # as long as you are also using SSL (which you should anyway).
    import Phoenix.LiveDashboard.Router

    scope "/dev" do
      pipe_through :browser

      live_dashboard "/dashboard", metrics: TodoListWeb.Telemetry
      forward "/mailbox", Plug.Swoosh.MailboxPreview
    end
  end
end
```

Make these changes:
1. Add `import PhoenixStorybook.Router` right after the `use TodoListWeb, :router` line.
2. Add a new scope block for storybook_assets BEFORE the main scope. Add it right before `scope "/", TodoListWeb do`:
```elixir
  scope "/" do
    storybook_assets()
  end
```
3. Inside the `if Application.compile_env(:todo_list, :dev_routes) do` block, add the storybook route alongside the existing dev routes. Add it inside the existing `/dev` scope:
```elixir
      live_storybook "/storybook", backend_module: TodoListWeb.Storybook
```

Read the file first, then make the edits.

  use TodoListWeb, :router

  pipeline :browser do
    plug :accepts, ["html"]
    plug :fetch_session
    plug :fetch_live_flash
    plug :put_root_layout, html: {TodoListWeb.Layouts, :root}
    plug :protect_from_forgery
    plug :put_secure_browser_headers
  end

  pipeline :api do
    plug :accepts, ["json"]
  end

  scope "/" do
    storybook_assets()
  end

  scope "/", TodoListWeb do
    pipe_through :browser

    get "/", PageController, :home
  end

  # Other scopes may use custom stacks.
  # scope "/api", TodoListWeb do
  #   pipe_through :api
  # end

  # Enable LiveDashboard and Swoosh mailbox preview in development
  if Application.compile_env(:todo_list, :dev_routes) do
    # If you want to use the LiveDashboard in production, you should put
    # it behind authentication and allow only admins to access it.
    # If your application does not have an admins-only section yet,
    # you can use Plug.BasicAuth to set up some basic authentication

    scope "/dev" do

      live_dashboard "/dashboard", metrics: TodoListWeb.Telemetry
      forward "/mailbox", Plug.Swoosh.MailboxPreview
      live_storybook "/storybook", backend_module: TodoListWeb.Storybook
    end

end

# This file is responsible for configuring your application
# and its dependencies with the aid of the Config module.
#
# This configuration file is loaded before any dependency and
# is restricted to this project.

# General application configuration
import Config

config :todo_list,
  ecto_repos: [TodoList.Repo],
  generators: [timestamp_type: :utc_datetime]

# Configure the endpoint
config :todo_list, TodoListWeb.Endpoint,
  url: [host: "localhost"],
  adapter: Bandit.PhoenixAdapter,
  render_errors: [
    formats: [html: TodoListWeb.ErrorHTML, json: TodoListWeb.ErrorJSON],
    layout: false
  ],
  pubsub_server: TodoList.PubSub,
  live_view: [signing_salt: "d+Zxmuln"]

# Configure the mailer
#
# By default it uses the "Local" adapter which stores the emails
# locally. You can see the emails in your browser, at "/dev/mailbox".
#
# For production it's recommended to configure a different adapter
# at the `config/runtime.exs`.
config :todo_list, TodoList.Mailer, adapter: Swoosh.Adapters.Local

# Configure esbuild (the version is required)
config :esbuild,
  version: "0.25.4",
  todo_list: [
    args:
      ~w(js/app.js --bundle --target=es2022 --outdir=../priv/static/assets/js --external:/fonts/* --external:/images/* --alias:@=.),
    cd: Path.expand("../assets", __DIR__),
    env: %{"NODE_PATH" => [Path.expand("../deps", __DIR__), Mix.Project.build_path()]}
  ]

# Configure tailwind (the version is required)
config :tailwind,
  version: "4.1.12",
  todo_list: [
    args: ~w(
      --input=assets/css/app.css
      --output=priv/static/assets/css/app.css
    ),
    cd: Path.expand("..", __DIR__)
  ]

# Configure Elixir's Logger
config :logger, :default_formatter,
  format: "$time $metadata[$level] $message\n",
  metadata: [:request_id]

# Use Jason for JSON parsing in Phoenix
config :phoenix, :json_library, Jason

# Import environment specific config. This must remain at the bottom
# of this file so it overrides the configuration defined above.
import_config "#{config_env()}.exs"

import Config

# Configure your database
config :todo_list, TodoList.Repo,
  username: "postgres",
  password: "postgres",
  hostname: "localhost",
  database: "todo_list_dev",
  stacktrace: true,
  show_sensitive_data_on_connection_error: true,
  pool_size: 10

# For development, we disable any cache and enable
# debugging and code reloading.
#
# The watchers configuration can be used to run external
# watchers to your application. For example, we can use it
# to bundle .js and .css sources.
config :todo_list, TodoListWeb.Endpoint,
  # Binding to loopback ipv4 address prevents access from other machines.
  # Change to `ip: {0, 0, 0, 0}` to allow access from other machines.
  http: [ip: {127, 0, 0, 1}],
  check_origin: false,
  code_reloader: true,
  debug_errors: true,
  secret_key_base: "EdSBLnLGx9tw6anVNqA5Njc6ds39dHt0wGC7HNlLWKFtzSE3jobumSQGxjTPCHeY",
  watchers: [
    esbuild: {Esbuild, :install_and_run, [:todo_list, ~w(--sourcemap=inline --watch)]},
    tailwind: {Tailwind, :install_and_run, [:todo_list, ~w(--watch)]}
  ]

# ## SSL Support
#
# In order to use HTTPS in development, a self-signed
# certificate can be generated by running the following
# Mix task:
#
#     mix phx.gen.cert
#
# Run `mix help phx.gen.cert` for more information.
#
# The `http:` config above can be replaced with:
#
#     https: [
#       port: 4001,
#       cipher_suite: :strong,
#       keyfile: "priv/cert/selfsigned_key.pem",
#       certfile: "priv/cert/selfsigned.pem"
#     ],
#
# If desired, both `http:` and `https:` keys can be
# configured to run both http and https servers on
# different ports.

# Reload browser tabs when matching files change.
config :todo_list, TodoListWeb.Endpoint,
  live_reload: [
    web_console_logger: true,
    patterns: [
      # Static assets, except user uploads
      ~r"priv/static/(?!uploads/).*\.(js|css|png|jpeg|jpg|gif|svg)$",
      # Gettext translations
      ~r"priv/gettext/.*\.po$",
      # Router, Controllers, LiveViews and LiveComponents
      ~r"lib/todo_list_web/router\.ex$",
      ~r"lib/todo_list_web/(controllers|live|components)/.*\.(ex|heex)$"
    ]
  ]

# Enable dev routes for dashboard and mailbox
config :todo_list, dev_routes: true

# Do not include metadata nor timestamps in development logs
config :logger, :default_formatter, format: "[$level] $message\n"

# Set a higher stacktrace during development. Avoid configuring such
# in production as building large stacktraces may be expensive.
config :phoenix, :stacktrace_depth, 20

# Initialize plugs at runtime for faster development compilation
config :phoenix, :plug_init_mode, :runtime

config :phoenix_live_view,
  # Include debug annotations and locations in rendered markup.
  # Changing this configuration will require mix clean and a full recompile.
  debug_heex_annotations: true,
  debug_attributes: true,
  # Enable helpful, but potentially expensive runtime checks
  enable_expensive_runtime_checks: true

# Disable swoosh api client as it is only required for production adapters.
config :swoosh, :api_client, false

input.old_string: ~r"lib/todo_list_web/(controllers|live|components)/.*\.(ex|heex)$"
input.new_string:
~r"lib/todo_list_web/(controllers|live|components)/.*\.(ex|heex)$",
      # Storybook stories
      ~r"storybook/.*\.(exs)$"

file.content:
defmodule TodoList.MixProject do
  use Mix.Project

  def project do
    [
      app: :todo_list,
      version: "0.1.0",
      elixir: "~> 1.15",
      elixirc_paths: elixirc_paths(Mix.env()),
      start_permanent: Mix.env() == :prod,
      aliases: aliases(),
      deps: deps(),
      compilers: [:phoenix_live_view] ++ Mix.compilers(),
      listeners: [Phoenix.CodeReloader]
    ]
  end

  # Configuration for the OTP application.
  #
  # Type `mix help compile.app` for more information.
  def application do
    [
      mod: {TodoList.Application, []},
      extra_applications: [:logger, :runtime_tools]
    ]
  end

  def cli do
    [
      preferred_envs: [precommit: :test]
    ]
  end

  # Specifies which paths to compile per environment.
  defp elixirc_paths(:test), do: ["lib", "test/support"]
  defp elixirc_paths(_), do: ["lib"]

  # Specifies your project dependencies.
  #
  # Type `mix help deps` for examples and options.
  defp deps do
    [
      {:phoenix, "~> 1.8.3"},
      {:phoenix_ecto, "~> 4.5"},
      {:ecto_sql, "~> 3.13"},
      {:postgrex, ">= 0.0.0"},
      {:phoenix_html, "~> 4.1"},
      {:phoenix_live_reload, "~> 1.2", only: :dev},
      {:phoenix_live_view, "~> 1.1.0"},
      {:lazy_html, ">= 0.1.0", only: :test},

Create a new file at `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/lib/todo_list_web/components/task_components.ex`. This module contains all task-specific UI components following the Atomic Design hierarchy from the design system. These are visual-only implementations for storybook preview — they render HTML with daisyUI/Tailwind classes but are not yet wired to backend logic. The module should `use Phoenix.Component` and reference `TodoListWeb.CoreComponents` where needed. Here is the complete module to write: ```elixir defmodule TodoListWeb.TaskComponents do @moduledoc """ Task-specific UI components organized by Atomic Design level. ## Atoms Simple, indivisible UI building blocks: `badge/1`, `drag_handle/1`, `empty_state_message/1`, `task_description_text/1`, `strikethrough_text/1`. ## Molecules Small groups of atoms functioning as a unit: `task_input/1`, `complete_checkbox/1`, `reopen_button/1`, `delete_button/1`, `confirm_delete_dialog/1`, `inline_edit_field/1`. ## Organisms Complex composed sections: `add_task_form/1`, `active_task_item/1`, `completed_task_item/1`, `active_task_list/1`, `completed_task_list/1`, `task_page_header/1`. """ use Phoenix.Component import TodoListWeb.CoreComponents, only: [icon: 1, button: 1, input: 1] alias Phoenix.LiveView.JS # ────────────────────────────────────────────── # Atoms # ────────────────────────────────────────────── @doc """ Renders a small pill badge displaying a numeric count. ## Examples <.badge count={3} /> """ attr :count, :integer, required: true attr :class, :any, default: nil def badge(assigns) do ~H""" {@count} """ end @doc """ Renders a drag handle grip affordance for reorderable items. ## Examples <.drag_handle /> """ attr :class, :any, default: nil def drag_handle(assigns) do ~H""" <.icon name="hero-bars-3" class="size-5" /> """ end @doc """ Renders centered, muted text for empty list states. ## Examples <.empty_state_message message="No tasks yet. Add one above!" /> """ attr :message, :string, required: true def empty_state_message(assigns) do ~H"""

{@message}

""" end @doc """ Renders a task description as plain text. ## Examples <.task_description_text description="Buy groceries" /> """ attr :description, :string, required: true attr :class, :any, default: nil def task_description_text(assigns) do ~H""" {@description} """ end @doc """ Renders a task description with strikethrough for completed tasks. ## Examples <.strikethrough_text description="Buy groceries" /> """ attr :description, :string, required: true attr :class, :any, default: nil def strikethrough_text(assigns) do ~H""" {@description} """ end # ────────────────────────────────────────────── # Molecules # ────────────────────────────────────────────── @doc """ Renders a text input with add button for creating new tasks. ## Examples <.task_input form={@form} /> """ attr :form, :any, default: nil attr :placeholder, :string, default: "What needs to be done?" def task_input(assigns) do ~H"""
""" end @doc """ Renders a checkbox for completing an active task. ## Examples <.complete_checkbox task_id="task-001" /> """ attr :task_id, :string, required: true def complete_checkbox(assigns) do ~H""" """ end @doc """ Renders a button to reopen a completed task. ## Examples <.reopen_button task_id="task-001" /> """ attr :task_id, :string, required: true def reopen_button(assigns) do ~H""" """ end @doc """ Renders a delete button that opens a confirmation dialog. ## Examples <.delete_button task_id="task-001" /> """ attr :task_id, :string, required: true def delete_button(assigns) do ~H""" """ end @doc """ Renders a confirmation dialog for task deletion. ## Examples <.confirm_delete_dialog task_id="task-001" description="Buy groceries" /> """ attr :task_id, :string, required: true attr :description, :string, required: true def confirm_delete_dialog(assigns) do ~H"""

Delete Task?

"{@description}" will be permanently removed.

 """ end @doc """ Renders an inline text input for editing a task description. ## Examples <.inline_edit_field task_id="task-001" description="Buy groceries" /> """ attr :task_id, :string, required: true attr :description, :string, required: true def inline_edit_field(assigns) do ~H""" """ end # ────────────────────────────────────────────── # Organisms # ────────────────────────────────────────────── @doc """ Renders the add task form with input and submit button. ## Examples <.add_task_form /> """ attr :form, :any, default: nil def add_task_form(assigns) do ~H"""
<.task_input form={@form} />
""" end @doc """ Renders a single active task item with drag handle, description, checkbox, and delete. ## Examples <.active_task_item task_id="task-001" description="Buy groceries" editing={false} /> """ attr :task_id, :string, required: true attr :description, :string, required: true attr :editing, :boolean, default: false def active_task_item(assigns) do ~H"""
<.drag_handle />
<.inline_edit_field :if={@editing} task_id={@task_id} description={@description} /> <.task_description_text description={@description} />
<.complete_checkbox task_id={@task_id} /> <.delete_button task_id={@task_id} /> <.confirm_delete_dialog task_id={@task_id} description={@description} />
""" end @doc """ Renders a single completed task item with strikethrough, reopen, and delete. ## Examples <.completed_task_item task_id="task-001" description="Buy groceries" /> """ attr :task_id, :string, required: true attr :description, :string, required: true def completed_task_item(assigns) do ~H"""
<.strikethrough_text description={@description} />
<.reopen_button task_id={@task_id} /> <.delete_button task_id={@task_id} /> <.confirm_delete_dialog task_id={@task_id} description={@description} />
""" end @doc """ Renders a list of active tasks or an empty state message. Accepts a list of task maps with `:id` and `:description` keys. ## Examples <.active_task_list tasks={[%{id: "1", description: "Buy milk"}]} editing_task_id={nil} /> <.active_task_list tasks={[]} editing_task_id={nil} /> """ attr :tasks, :list, required: true attr :editing_task_id, :string, default: nil def active_task_list(assigns) do ~H"""
<.empty_state_message message="No tasks yet. Add one above!" />
<.active_task_item task_id={task.id} description={task.description} editing={@editing_task_id == task.id} />
""" end @doc """ Renders a list of completed tasks or an empty state message. Accepts a list of task maps with `:id` and `:description` keys. ## Examples <.completed_task_list tasks={[%{id: "1", description: "Buy milk"}]} /> <.completed_task_list tasks={[]} /> """ attr :tasks, :list, required: true def completed_task_list(assigns) do ~H"""
<.empty_state_message message="No completed tasks." />
<.completed_task_item task_id={task.id} description={task.description} />
""" end @doc """ Renders the task page header. Two variants based on `:variant` attribute. ## Variants - `:active` — "My Tasks" title with completed count badge link - `:completed` — "< Back to Tasks" link with "Completed Tasks" title ## Examples <.task_page_header variant={:active} completed_count={3} /> <.task_page_header variant={:completed} /> """ attr :variant, :atom, values: [:active, :completed], required: true attr :completed_count, :integer, default: 0 def task_page_header(assigns) do ~H"""

My Tasks

Completed <.badge count={@completed_count} />
<.icon name="hero-arrow-left" class="size-4" /> Back to Tasks

Completed Tasks

""" end end ``` Write this exact content to the file.

1→defmodule TodoListWeb.CoreComponents do
     2→  @moduledoc """
     3→  Provides core UI components.
     4→
     5→  At first glance, this module may seem daunting, but its goal is to provide
     6→  core building blocks for your application, such as tables, forms, and
     7→  inputs. The components consist mostly of markup and are well-documented
     8→  with doc strings and declarative assigns. You may customize and style
     9→  them in any way you want, based on your application growth and needs.
    10→
    11→  The foundation for styling is Tailwind CSS, a utility-first CSS framework,
    12→  augmented with daisyUI, a Tailwind CSS plugin that provides UI components
    13→  and themes. Here are useful references:
    14→
    15→    * [daisyUI](https://daisyui.com/docs/intro/) - a good place to get
    16→      started and see the available components.
    17→
    18→    * [Tailwind CSS](https://tailwindcss.com) - the foundational framework
    19→      we build on. You will use it for layout, sizing, flexbox, grid, and
    20→      spacing.
    21→
    22→    * [Heroicons](https://heroicons.com) - see `icon/1` for usage.
    23→
    24→    * [Phoenix.Component](https://hexdocs.pm/phoenix_live_view/Phoenix.Component.html) -
    25→      the component system used by Phoenix. Some components, such as `<.link>`
    26→      and `<.form>`, are defined there.
    27→
    28→  """
    29→  use Phoenix.Component
    30→  use Gettext, backend: TodoListWeb.Gettext

1→defmodule TodoListWeb.TaskComponents do 2→ @moduledoc """ 3→ Task-specific UI components organized by Atomic Design level. 4→ 5→ ## Atoms 6→ Simple, indivisible UI building blocks: `badge/1`, `drag_handle/1`, 7→ `empty_state_message/1`, `task_description_text/1`, `strikethrough_text/1`. 8→ 9→ ## Molecules 10→ Small groups of atoms functioning as a unit: `task_input/1`, `complete_checkbox/1`, 11→ `reopen_button/1`, `delete_button/1`, `confirm_delete_dialog/1`, `inline_edit_field/1`. 12→ 13→ ## Organisms 14→ Complex composed sections: `add_task_form/1`, `active_task_item/1`, 15→ `completed_task_item/1`, `active_task_list/1`, `completed_task_list/1`, 16→ `task_page_header/1`. 17→ """ 18→ use Phoenix.Component 19→ 20→ import TodoListWeb.CoreComponents, only: [icon: 1] 21→ 22→ alias Phoenix.LiveView.JS 23→ 24→ # ────────────────────────────────────────────── 25→ # Atoms 26→ # ────────────────────────────────────────────── 27→ 28→ @doc """ 29→ Renders a small pill badge displaying a numeric count. 30→ 31→ ## Examples 32→ 33→ <.badge count={3} /> 34→ """ 35→ attr :count, :integer, required: true 36→ attr :class, :any, default: nil 37→ 38→ def badge(assigns) do 39→ ~H""" 40→ {@count} 41→ """ 42→ end 43→ 44→ @doc """ 45→ Renders a drag handle grip affordance for reorderable items. 46→ 47→ ## Examples 48→ 49→ <.drag_handle /> 50→ """ 51→ attr :class, :any, default: nil 52→ 53→ def drag_handle(assigns) do 54→ ~H""" 55→ 59→ <.icon name="hero-bars-3" class="size-5" /> 60→ 61→ """ 62→ end 63→ 64→ @doc """ 65→ Renders centered, muted text for empty list states. 66→ 67→ ## Examples 68→ 69→ <.empty_state_message message="No tasks yet. Add one above!" /> 70→ """ 71→ attr :message, :string, required: true 72→ 73→ def empty_state_message(assigns) do 74→ ~H""" 75→

{@message}

76→ """ 77→ end 78→ 79→ @doc """ 80→ Renders a task description as plain text. 81→ 82→ ## Examples 83→ 84→ <.task_description_text description="Buy groceries" /> 85→ """ 86→ attr :description, :string, required: true 87→ attr :class, :any, default: nil 88→ 89→ def task_description_text(assigns) do 90→ ~H""" 91→ {@description} 92→ """ 93→ end 94→ 95→ @doc """ 96→ Renders a task description with strikethrough for completed tasks. 97→ 98→ ## Examples 99→ 100→ <.strikethrough_text description="Buy groceries" /> 101→ """ 102→ attr :description, :string, required: true 103→ attr :class, :any, default: nil 104→ 105→ def strikethrough_text(assigns) do 106→ ~H""" 107→ {@description} 108→ """ 109→ end 110→ 111→ # ────────────────────────────────────────────── 112→ # Molecules 113→ # ────────────────────────────────────────────── 114→ 115→ @doc """ 116→ Renders a text input with add button for creating new tasks. 117→ 118→ ## Examples 119→ 120→ <.task_input form={@form} /> 121→ """ 122→ attr :form, :any, default: nil 123→ attr :placeholder, :string, default: "What needs to be done?" 124→ 125→ def task_input(assigns) do 126→ ~H""" 127→
128→ 135→ 138→
139→ """ 140→ end 141→ 142→ @doc """ 143→ Renders a checkbox for completing an active task. 144→ 145→ ## Examples 146→ 147→ <.complete_checkbox task_id="task-001" /> 148→ """ 149→ attr :task_id, :string, required: true 150→ 151→ def complete_checkbox(assigns) do 152→ ~H""" 153→ 160→ """ 161→ end 162→ 163→ @doc """ 164→ Renders a button to reopen a completed task. 165→ 166→ ## Examples 167→ 168→ <.reopen_button task_id="task-001" /> 169→ """ 170→ attr :task_id, :string, required: true 171→ 172→ def reopen_button(assigns) do 173→ ~H""" 174→ 177→ """ 178→ end 179→ 180→ @doc """ 181→ Renders a delete button that opens a confirmation dialog. 182→ 183→ ## Examples 184→ 185→ <.delete_button task_id="task-001" /> 186→ """ 187→ attr :task_id, :string, required: true 188→ 189→ def delete_button(assigns) do 190→ ~H""" 191→ 198→ """ 199→ end 200→ 201→ @doc """ 202→ Renders a confirmation dialog for task deletion. 203→ 204→ ## Examples 205→ 206→ <.confirm_delete_dialog task_id="task-001" description="Buy groceries" /> 207→ """ 208→ attr :task_id, :string, required: true 209→ attr :description, :string, required: true 210→ 211→ def confirm_delete_dialog(assigns) do 212→ ~H""" 213→ 214→

defmodule TodoListWeb.Router do
  use TodoListWeb, :router
  import PhoenixStorybook.Router

  pipeline :browser do
    plug :accepts, ["html"]
    plug :fetch_session
    plug :fetch_live_flash
    plug :put_root_layout, html: {TodoListWeb.Layouts, :root}
    plug :protect_from_forgery
    plug :put_secure_browser_headers
  end

  pipeline :api do
    plug :accepts, ["json"]
  end

  scope "/" do
    storybook_assets()
  end

  scope "/", TodoListWeb do
    pipe_through :browser

    get "/", PageController, :home
  end

  # Other scopes may use custom stacks.
  # scope "/api", TodoListWeb do
  #   pipe_through :api
  # end

  # Enable LiveDashboard and Swoosh mailbox preview in development
  if Application.compile_env(:todo_list, :dev_routes) do
    # If you want to use the LiveDashboard in production, you should put
    # it behind authentication and allow only admins to access it.
    # If your application does not have an admins-only section yet,
    # you can use Plug.BasicAuth to set up some basic authentication
    # as long as you are also using SSL (which you should anyway).
    import Phoenix.LiveDashboard.Router

    scope "/dev" do
      pipe_through :browser

      live_dashboard "/dashboard", metrics: TodoListWeb.Telemetry
      forward "/mailbox", Plug.Swoosh.MailboxPreview
      live_storybook "/storybook", backend_module: TodoListWeb.Storybook
    end
  end
end

if Application.compile_env(:todo_list, :dev_routes) do
    # If you want to use the LiveDashboard in production, you should put
    # it behind authentication and allow only admins to access it.
    # If your application does not have an admins-only section yet,
    # you can use Plug.BasicAuth to set up some basic authentication
    # as long as you are also using SSL (which you should anyway).
    import Phoenix.LiveDashboard.Router

    scope "/dev" do
      pipe_through :browser

      live_dashboard "/dashboard", metrics: TodoListWeb.Telemetry
      forward "/mailbox", Plug.Swoosh.MailboxPreview
    end

    if Code.ensure_loaded?(PhoenixStorybook.Router) do
      import PhoenixStorybook.Router

      scope "/" do
        storybook_assets()
      end

      scope "/dev" do
        pipe_through :browser
        live_storybook "/storybook", backend_module: TodoListWeb.Storybook
      end

defmodule TodoListWeb.Router do
  use TodoListWeb, :router

  pipeline :browser do
    plug :accepts, ["html"]
    plug :fetch_session
    plug :fetch_live_flash
    plug :put_root_layout, html: {TodoListWeb.Layouts, :root}
    plug :protect_from_forgery
    plug :put_secure_browser_headers
  end

  pipeline :api do
    plug :accepts, ["json"]
  end

  scope "/", TodoListWeb do
    pipe_through :browser

    get "/", PageController, :home
  end

  # Other scopes may use custom stacks.
  # scope "/api", TodoListWeb do
  #   pipe_through :api
  # end

  # Enable LiveDashboard and Swoosh mailbox preview in development
  if Application.compile_env(:todo_list, :dev_routes) do
    # If you want to use the LiveDashboard in production, you should put
    # it behind authentication and allow only admins to access it.
    # If your application does not have an admins-only section yet,
    # you can use Plug.BasicAuth to set up some basic authentication
    # as long as you are also using SSL (which you should anyway).
    import Phoenix.LiveDashboard.Router

    scope "/dev" do
      pipe_through :browser

      live_dashboard "/dashboard", metrics: TodoListWeb.Telemetry
      forward "/mailbox", Plug.Swoosh.MailboxPreview
    end

    if Code.ensure_loaded?(PhoenixStorybook.Router) do
      import PhoenixStorybook.Router

      scope "/" do
        storybook_assets()
      end

      scope "/dev" do
        pipe_through :browser
        live_storybook "/storybook", backend_module: TodoListWeb.Storybook
      end
    end
  end
end

BUT - there's a catch. In Elixir, `import` is a compile-time directive. 

The real issue is that `Application.compile_env` isn't a literal value, so the `if` macro can't do dead-code elimination the way it would with a literal false. The compiler still needs to parse and understand the body to check for compilation errors, even if the condition evaluates to false at compile time. That's why the import error is still being raised. So the dashboard modules are available in test since `phoenix_live_dashboard` isn't restricted to dev, which means the import succeeds even though the conditional should skip it. import Config

# Configure your database
#
# The MIX_TEST_PARTITION environment variable can be used
# to provide built-in test partitioning in CI environment.
# Run `mix help test` for more information.
config :todo_list, TodoList.Repo,
  username: "postgres",
  password: "postgres",
  hostname: "localhost",
  database: "todo_list_test#{System.get_env("MIX_TEST_PARTITION")}",
  pool: Ecto.Adapters.SQL.Sandbox,
  pool_size: System.schedulers_online() * 2

# We don't run a server during test. If one is required,
# you can enable the server option below.
config :todo_list, TodoListWeb.Endpoint,
  http: [ip: {127, 0, 0, 1}, port: 4002],
  secret_key_base: "UZOdotzn/Z5FwbHhrPEB5VMaqE22rKeJfstXaQzglLZzBoHhCO9VIRSZ2NIGpvyT",
  server: false

# In test we don't send emails
config :todo_list, TodoList.Mailer, adapter: Swoosh.Adapters.Test

# Disable swoosh api client as it is only required for production adapters
config :swoosh, :api_client, false

# Print only warnings and errors during test
config :logger, level: :warning

# Initialize plugs at runtime for faster test compilation
config :phoenix, :plug_init_mode, :runtime

# Enable helpful, but potentially expensive runtime checks
config :phoenix_live_view,
  enable_expensive_runtime_checks: true

# Sort query params output of verified routes for robust url comparisons
config :phoenix,
  sort_verified_routes_query_params: true

toolUseResult.originalFile:
defmodule TodoListWeb.Router do
  use TodoListWeb, :router

  pipeline :browser do
    plug :accepts, ["html"]
    plug :fetch_session
    plug :fetch_live_flash
    plug :put_root_layout, html: {TodoListWeb.Layouts, :root}
    plug :protect_from_forgery
    plug :put_secure_browser_headers
  end

  pipeline :api do
    plug :accepts, ["json"]
  end

  scope "/", TodoListWeb do
    pipe_through :browser

    get "/", PageController, :home
  end

  # Other scopes may use custom stacks.
  # scope "/api", TodoListWeb do
  #   pipe_through :api
  # end

  # Enable LiveDashboard and Swoosh mailbox preview in development
  if Application.compile_env(:todo_list, :dev_routes) do
    # If you want to use the LiveDashboard in production, you should put
    # it behind authentication and allow only admins to access it.
    # If your application does not have an admins-only section yet,
    # you can use Plug.BasicAuth to set up some basic authentication
    # as long as you are also using SSL (which you should anyway).
    import Phoenix.LiveDashboard.Router

    scope "/dev" do
      pipe_through :browser

      live_dashboard "/dashboard", metrics: TodoListWeb.Telemetry
      forward "/mailbox", Plug.Swoosh.MailboxPreview
    end

    if Code.ensure_loaded?(PhoenixStorybook.Router) do
      import PhoenixStorybook.Router

      scope "/" do
        storybook_assets()
      end

      scope "/dev" do
        pipe_through :browser
        live_storybook "/storybook", backend_module: TodoListWeb.Storybook
      end
    end
  end
end

  defmodule TodoListWeb.Storybook do
    use PhoenixStorybook,
      otp_app: :todo_list,

      css_path: "/assets/storybook.css",
      js_path: "/assets/storybook.js",
      sandbox_class: "todo-list"
  end
end
