input.prompt:
Update the Theme Configuration section (section 10) in `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md` to reflect the new Catppuccin color themes.

Replace the entire section 10 ("## 10. Theme Configuration") and everything after it through the end of file, with this new content:

```markdown
## 10. Theme Configuration

Both themes are defined in `assets/css/app.css` using daisyUI's theme plugin with OKLch color values derived from the [Catppuccin](https://catppuccin.com/) palette. The active theme is set via the `data-theme` attribute on the root `<html>` element.

### Dark Theme (Catppuccin Mocha)

Catppuccin Mocha — the darkest flavor, offering a cozy feeling with color-rich accents. Default when user's system preference is dark.

| Token | Catppuccin Color | Hex | Usage |
|-------|-----------------|-----|-------|
| `base-100` | Base | `#1e1e2e` | Main background |
| `base-200` | Mantle | `#181825` | Card/section background |
| `base-300` | Crust | `#11111b` | Deeper background, borders |
| `base-content` | Text | `#cdd6f4` | Primary text |
| `primary` | Mauve | `#cba6f7` | Buttons, active states, accents |
| `primary-content` | Base | `#1e1e2e` | Text on primary background |
| `secondary` | Pink | `#f5c2e7` | Secondary actions |
| `secondary-content` | Base | `#1e1e2e` | Text on secondary background |
| `accent` | Rosewater | `#f5e0dc` | Accent highlights |
| `accent-content` | Base | `#1e1e2e` | Text on accent background |
| `neutral` | Surface 0 | `#313244` | Neutral backgrounds |
| `neutral-content` | Text | `#cdd6f4` | Text on neutral background |
| `info` | Sky | `#89dceb` | Informational states |
| `success` | Green | `#a6e3a1` | Success states |
| `warning` | Yellow | `#f9e2af` | Warning states |
| `error` | Red | `#f38ba8` | Error states |

### Light Theme (Catppuccin Latte)

Catppuccin Latte — the lightest flavor, a warm daytime companion. Default theme.

| Token | Catppuccin Color | Hex | Usage |
|-------|-----------------|-----|-------|
| `base-100` | Base | `#eff1f5` | Main background |
| `base-200` | Mantle | `#e6e9ef` | Card/section background |
| `base-300` | Crust | `#dce0e8` | Deeper background, borders |
| `base-content` | Text | `#4c4f69` | Primary text |
| `primary` | Mauve | `#8839ef` | Buttons, active states, accents |
| `primary-content` | Base | `#eff1f5` | Text on primary background |
| `secondary` | Pink | `#ea76cb` | Secondary actions |
| `secondary-content` | Base | `#eff1f5` | Text on secondary background |
| `accent` | Rosewater | `#dc8a78` | Accent highlights |
| `accent-content` | Base | `#eff1f5` | Text on accent background |
| `neutral` | Crust | `#dce0e8` | Neutral backgrounds |
| `neutral-content` | Text | `#4c4f69` | Text on neutral background |
| `info` | Sky | `#04a5e5` | Informational states |
| `success` | Green | `#40a02b` | Success states |
| `warning` | Yellow | `#df8e1d` | Warning states |
| `error` | Red | `#d20f39` | Error states |

### Shared Design Tokens

Both themes share these dimensional tokens:

| Token | Value | Usage |
|-------|-------|-------|
| `--radius-selector` | `0.25rem` | Selectors, small controls |
| `--radius-field` | `0.25rem` | Input fields |
| `--radius-box` | `0.5rem` | Cards, containers |
| `--border` | `1.5px` | Border width |
```

Read the file first, then replace section 10 and everything after it.

1→# Design System 2→ 3→Brad Frost's Atomic Design methodology applied to a Phoenix LiveView todo list application. The UI uses Tailwind CSS with daisyUI for component styling and theming, Heroicons for iconography, and Phoenix LiveView streams for efficient list rendering. 4→ 5→The application supports light and dark themes via the `data-theme` attribute on the root HTML element. Theme switching is handled by the existing `theme_toggle/1` component in `Layouts`. 6→ 7→Generic UI primitives (flash messages, buttons, inputs, headers, icons, show/hide transitions) already exist in `CoreComponents` (`lib/todo_list_web/components/core_components.ex`). This document defines the **task-specific component hierarchy** that builds on top of those existing components. 8→ 9→--- 10→ 11→## 1. Atoms 12→ 13→The smallest, indivisible UI building blocks. 14→ 15→### Icon 16→ 17→Existing `CoreComponents.icon/1` wrapping Heroicons. Used throughout the task UI for: 18→ 19→- **Drag handle** -- `hero-bars-3` or a six-dot grip pattern 20→- **Checkmark** -- `hero-check` 21→- **Close/delete** -- `hero-x-mark` 22→- **Arrow left** -- `hero-arrow-left` 23→- **Plus** -- `hero-plus` 24→ 25→### Badge 26→ 27→Small pill displaying a numeric count. Uses daisyUI `badge badge-primary` class. Used for the completed task count in the page header (e.g., "Completed (3)"). 28→ 29→```heex 30→{@count} 31→``` 32→ 33→### TaskDescriptionText 34→ 35→Plain text rendering of a task description. Styled with `text-base-content` for theme-appropriate contrast. This is the default display mode for an active task's description. 36→ 37→```heex 38→{@description} 39→``` 40→ 41→### StrikethroughText 42→ 43→Completed task description with `line-through` text decoration. Visually communicates that the task is done. 44→ 45→```heex 46→{@description} 47→``` 48→ 49→### EmptyStateMessage 50→ 51→Centered, muted text displayed when a list contains no items. Used for both active and completed task lists. 52→ 53→- Active: "No tasks yet. Add one above!" 54→- Completed: "No completed tasks." 55→ 56→```heex 57→

{@message}

58→``` 59→ 60→### DragHandle 61→ 62→The grip affordance indicating a draggable item. Rendered as a six-dot grip icon or `hero-bars-3`. Cursor changes to `cursor-grab` at rest and `cursor-grabbing` while dragging. 63→ 64→```heex 65→ 66→ <.icon name="hero-bars-3" class="size-5" /> 67→ 68→``` 69→ 70→--- 71→ 72→## 2. Molecules 73→ 74→Small groups of atoms that function as a unit. 75→ 76→### TaskInput 77→ 78→Text input combined with a submit button for adding new tasks. Composed of an `input` atom (text field with placeholder "What needs to be done?") and a `button` atom ([+ Add]). Wrapped in a `<.form>` with `phx-submit`. 79→ 80→```heex 81→<.form for={@form} phx-submit="add_task" class="join w-full"> 82→ <.input 83→ field={@form[:description]} 84→ type="text" 85→ placeholder="What needs to be done?" 86→ class="input input-bordered join-item flex-1" 87→ /> 88→ <.button variant="primary" class="btn btn-primary join-item"> 89→ <.icon name="hero-plus" class="size-4" /> Add 90→ 91→ 92→``` 93→ 94→### CompleteCheckbox 95→ 96→Clickable checkbox that marks a task as complete. Fires `phx-click="complete_task"` with the task's ID. Visual state: unchecked square. 97→ 98→```heex 99→ 105→``` 106→ 107→### ReopenButton 108→ 109→Text button that reopens a completed task. Fires `phx-click="reopen_task"` with the task's ID. Styled as `btn-ghost btn-sm` for a subtle appearance. 110→ 111→```heex 112→ 115→``` 116→ 117→### DeleteButton 118→ 119→Icon button ([x]) that initiates the delete confirmation flow. Clicking opens the `ConfirmDeleteDialog`. Styled as `btn-ghost btn-sm` with error color on hover. 120→ 121→```heex 122→ 128→``` 129→ 130→### ConfirmDeleteDialog 131→ 132→Modal dialog confirming task deletion. Displays the task description in quotes, a warning message, and [Cancel] + [Delete] buttons. Uses daisyUI `modal` component. [Delete] is `btn-error`, [Cancel] is `btn-ghost`. 133→ 134→```heex 135→ 136→
137→

Delete Task?

138→

"{@description}" will be permanently removed.

139→
140→
141→ 142→
143→ 146→
147→
148→
149→ 150→``` 151→ 152→### InlineEditField 153→ 154→Text input that replaces `TaskDescriptionText` when the user clicks on a task's description. Auto-focuses on mount. Submits on Enter or blur, reverts on Escape. 155→ 156→```heex 157→ 167→``` 168→ 169→--- 170→ 171→## 3. Organisms 172→ 173→Complex, distinct sections of the UI composed of molecules. 174→ 175→### AddTaskForm 176→ 177→The full add-task row at the top of the active tasks view. Composed of the `TaskInput` molecule. Handles form submission via `phx-submit="add_task"`, clears the input on success, and displays validation errors on failure. 178→ 179→### ActiveTaskItem 180→ 181→A single row in the active task list. Composed of: 182→ 183→1. **DragHandle** -- grab target for reordering 184→2. **TaskDescriptionText** (display mode) or **InlineEditField** (edit mode) 185→3. **CompleteCheckbox** -- marks task as done 186→4. **DeleteButton** -- triggers delete confirmation 187→ 188→Clicking the description text enters edit mode (swaps `TaskDescriptionText` for `InlineEditField`). The item is draggable via the handle. Rendered within a LiveView stream for efficient DOM patching. 189→ 190→```heex 191→
192→ 193→
194→ 195→
196→ 197→ 198→
199→``` 200→ 201→### CompletedTaskItem 202→ 203→A single row in the completed task list. Simpler than `ActiveTaskItem` -- no drag handle, no inline edit, no checkbox. Composed of: 204→ 205→1. **StrikethroughText** -- the completed description 206→2. **ReopenButton** -- reopens the task 207→3. **DeleteButton** -- triggers delete confirmation 208→ 209→```heex 210→
211→
212→ 213→
214→ 215→ 216→
217→``` 218→ 219→### ActiveTaskList 220→ 221→Ordered container of `ActiveTaskItem` organisms. Uses `phx-update="stream"` for efficient DOM updates. Implements drag-and-drop reordering via a JS hook. When the stream is empty, renders `EmptyStateMessage` with "No tasks yet. Add one above!". 222→ 223→```heex 224→
225→
226→ <.active_task_item task={task} editing={@editing_task_id == task.id} /> 227→
228→
229→``` 230→ 231→### CompletedTaskList 232→ 233→Container of `CompletedTaskItem` organisms. Uses `phx-update="stream"`. When the stream is empty, renders `EmptyStateMessage` with "No completed tasks.". 234→ 235→```heex 236→
237→
238→ <.completed_task_item task={task} /> 239→
240→
241→``` 242→ 243→### TaskPageHeader 244→ 245→Header bar for task pages. Two variants: 246→ 247→**Active view:** "My Tasks" title on the left, [Completed (N)] navigation badge on the right. 248→ 249→```heex 250→
251→

My Tasks

252→ <.link navigate={~p"/completed"} class="btn btn-ghost btn-sm"> 253→ Completed {@completed_count} 254→ 255→
256→``` 257→ 258→**Completed view:** [< Back to Tasks] navigation link on the left, "Completed Tasks" title on the right. 259→ 260→```heex 261→
262→ <.link navigate={~p"/"} class="btn btn-ghost btn-sm"> 263→ <.icon name="hero-arrow-left" class="size-4" /> Back to Tasks 264→ 265→

Completed Tasks

266→
267→``` 268→ 269→--- 270→ 271→## 4. Templates 272→ 273→Page-level structural layouts defining the arrangement of organisms. Templates contain no real data -- they define the skeleton. 274→ 275→### ActiveTasksTemplate 276→ 277→Vertical layout: `TaskPageHeader` (active variant) followed by `AddTaskForm` followed by `ActiveTaskList` (or `EmptyStateMessage`). Content is in a max-width container, centered horizontally. 278→ 279→``` 280→+--------------------------------------------------+ 281→| TaskPageHeader (active variant) | 282→+--------------------------------------------------+ 283→| AddTaskForm | 284→+--------------------------------------------------+ 285→| ActiveTaskList | EmptyStateMessage | 286→+--------------------------------------------------+ 287→``` 288→ 289→### CompletedTasksTemplate 290→ 291→Vertical layout: `TaskPageHeader` (completed variant) followed by `CompletedTaskList` (or `EmptyStateMessage`). Same max-width container, centered. 292→ 293→``` 294→+--------------------------------------------------+ 295→| TaskPageHeader (completed variant) | 296→+--------------------------------------------------+ 297→| CompletedTaskList | EmptyStateMessage | 298→+--------------------------------------------------+ 299→``` 300→ 301→--- 302→ 303→## 5. Pages 304→ 305→Templates populated with real data from LiveView assigns. 306→ 307→### ActiveTasksPage 308→ 309→**LiveView module:** `TodoListWeb.TaskLive.Index` 310→**Route:** `/` 311→ 312→Subscribes to PubSub for real-time updates across browser tabs. 313→ 314→**Assigns:** 315→ 316→| Assign | Type | Description | 317→|--------|------|-------------| 318→| `@tasks` | stream | Active tasks ordered by priority | 319→| `@completed_count` | integer | Number of completed tasks (for badge) | 320→| `@form` | Phoenix.HTML.Form | Changeset form for add task input | 321→| `@editing_task_id` | string or nil | ID of the task currently in inline edit mode | 322→ 323→**Events handled:** 324→ 325→| Event | Action | 326→|-------|--------| 327→| `add_task` | Dispatch `AddTask` command, clear form on success | 328→| `complete_task` | Dispatch `CompleteTask` command | 329→| `delete_task` | Dispatch `DeleteTask` command, close confirmation dialog | 330→| `edit_task` | Set `@editing_task_id` to enter inline edit mode | 331→| `save_description` | Dispatch `ChangeTaskDescription` command, exit edit mode | 332→| `handle_edit_keydown` | Enter: save, Escape: cancel edit | 333→| `reorder` | Dispatch `ReprioritizeTask` command with new position | 334→ 335→### CompletedTasksPage 336→ 337→**LiveView module:** `TodoListWeb.TaskLive.Completed` 338→**Route:** `/completed` 339→ 340→Subscribes to PubSub for real-time updates. 341→ 342→**Assigns:** 343→ 344→| Assign | Type | Description | 345→|--------|------|-------------| 346→| `@tasks` | stream | Completed tasks ordered by completion date | 347→ 348→**Events handled:** 349→ 350→| Event | Action | 351→|-------|--------| 352→| `reopen_task` | Dispatch `ReopenTask` command | 353→| `delete_task` | Dispatch `DeleteTask` command, close confirmation dialog | 354→ 355→--- 356→ 357→## 6. Component-to-File Mapping 358→ 359→| Level | Component | File | 360→|-------|-----------|------| 361→| Atom | Icon, Button, Input | `core_components.ex` (existing) | 362→| Atom | Badge, DragHandle, EmptyStateMessage, TaskDescriptionText, StrikethroughText | `task_components.ex` | 363→| Molecule | TaskInput, CompleteCheckbox, ReopenButton, DeleteButton, ConfirmDeleteDialog, InlineEditField | `task_components.ex` | 364→| Organism | AddTaskForm, ActiveTaskItem, CompletedTaskItem, ActiveTaskList, CompletedTaskList, TaskPageHeader | `task_components.ex` | 365→| Template | ActiveTasksTemplate, CompletedTasksTemplate | Inline in LiveView `render/1` | 366→| Page | ActiveTasksPage, CompletedTasksPage | `task_live/index.ex`, `task_live/completed.ex` | 367→ 368→**File paths:** 369→ 370→- `lib/todo_list_web/components/core_components.ex` -- generic atoms (existing, do not modify) 371→- `lib/todo_list_web/components/task_components.ex` -- task-specific atoms, molecules, organisms (new) 372→- `lib/todo_list_web/live/task_live/index.ex` -- active tasks LiveView page (new) 373→- `lib/todo_list_web/live/task_live/completed.ex` -- completed tasks LiveView page (new) 374→ 375→--- 376→ 377→## 7. Interaction Patterns 378→ 379→### Drag-and-Drop Reordering 380→ 381→Implemented via a Phoenix LiveView JS hook (e.g., `SortableHook` using SortableJS or native HTML5 drag API). 382→ 383→1. The `DragHandle` atom is the only grab target (`[draggable]`). The `handle` option in the hook config restricts dragging to the handle element. 384→2. On drop, the hook pushes a `reorder` event to the LiveView with `{task_id, new_position}`. 385→3. The LiveView dispatches a `ReprioritizeTask` command to the aggregate. 386→4. The projection updates, and the stream reflects the new order. 387→ 388→No client-side optimism: the list re-renders after the event propagates through the event store and projection. 389→ 390→### Inline Editing 391→ 392→1. Click on `TaskDescriptionText` triggers `phx-click="edit_task"` with the task ID. 393→2. LiveView sets `@editing_task_id`, which swaps the text for `InlineEditField`. 394→3. `InlineEditField` auto-focuses on mount via `phx-mounted={JS.focus()}`. 395→4. **Enter key** (`phx-keydown`): submits the new description via `save_description` event. 396→5. **Blur** (`phx-blur`): submits the new description via `save_description` event. 397→6. **Escape key** (`phx-keydown`): cancels editing, reverts to display mode (sets `@editing_task_id` to nil). 398→7. LiveView dispatches `ChangeTaskDescription` command on submit. 399→8. No client-side optimism: waits for event to propagate through the projection before updating the display. 400→ 401→### Delete Confirmation 402→ 403→1. Click `DeleteButton` opens `ConfirmDeleteDialog` via `JS.show` or daisyUI modal toggle. 404→2. The dialog displays the task description in quotes with a warning ("will be permanently removed"). 405→3. **Cancel**: closes the dialog via JS command (no server roundtrip). 406→4. **Delete**: fires `phx-click="delete_task"` with the task ID. The LiveView dispatches a `DeleteTask` command. On success, the dialog closes and the task is removed from the stream. 407→ 408→--- 409→ 410→## 8. Accessibility 411→ 412→- All interactive elements have visible focus indicators (provided by daisyUI defaults). 413→- Delete confirmation prevents accidental deletions. 414→- Drag handle has `aria-grabbed` and `aria-dropeffect` attributes for screen reader support. 415→- Keyboard support: Enter and Escape in inline edit, Enter in add form, Tab navigation between controls. 416→- Screen reader semantics: task list uses `role="list"`, individual tasks use `role="listitem"`. 417→- Color contrast meets WCAG AA in both light and dark themes (daisyUI theme defaults). 418→- Empty states are rendered as text content (not just visual absence) so they are announced by screen readers. 419→ 420→--- 421→ 422→## 9. daisyUI Component Usage 423→ 424→| Our Component | daisyUI Base | Customization | 425→|---|---|---| 426→| Badge | `badge badge-primary` | Shows completed task count | 427→| TaskInput | `input input-bordered` | Combined with `join` for input+button group | 428→| Add Button | `btn btn-primary` | In `join` group with input | 429→| CompleteCheckbox | `checkbox checkbox-primary checkbox-sm` | Unchecked by default | 430→| ReopenButton | `btn btn-ghost btn-sm` | Text only, no icon | 431→| DeleteButton | `btn btn-ghost btn-sm` | `hover:btn-error` for color on hover | 432→| ConfirmDeleteDialog | `modal` + `modal-box` | Standard daisyUI modal pattern | 433→| ActiveTaskItem | `flex items-center gap-2` | Custom flex row with `rounded-box bg-base-200` | 434→| TaskPageHeader | `flex justify-between items-center` | Custom header layout | 435→ 436→--- 437→ 438→## 10. Theme Configuration 439→ 440→Both themes are defined in `assets/css/app.css` using daisyUI's theme plugin with OKLch color values.
```markdown
## 10. Theme Configuration

Both themes are defined in `assets/css/app.css` using daisyUI's theme plugin with OKLch color values derived from the [Catppuccin](https://catppuccin.com/) palette. The active theme is set via the `data-theme` attribute on the root `<html>` element.

### Dark Theme (Catppuccin Mocha)

Catppuccin Mocha — the darkest flavor, offering a cozy feeling with color-rich accents. Default when user's system preference is dark.

| Token | Catppuccin Color | Hex | Usage |
|-------|-----------------|-----|-------|
| `base-100` | Base | `#1e1e2e` | Main background |
| `base-200` | Mantle | `#181825` | Card/section background |
| `base-300` | Crust | `#11111b` | Deeper background, borders |
| `base-content` | Text | `#cdd6f4` | Primary text |
| `primary` | Mauve | `#cba6f7` | Buttons, active states, accents |
| `primary-content` | Base | `#1e1e2e` | Text on primary background |
| `secondary` | Pink | `#f5c2e7` | Secondary actions |
| `secondary-content` | Base | `#1e1e2e` | Text on secondary background |
| `accent` | Rosewater | `#f5e0dc` | Accent highlights |
| `accent-content` | Base | `#1e1e2e` | Text on accent background |
| `neutral` | Surface 0 | `#313244` | Neutral backgrounds |
| `neutral-content` | Text | `#cdd6f4` | Text on neutral background |
| `info` | Sky | `#89dceb` | Informational states |
| `success` | Green | `#a6e3a1` | Success states |
| `warning` | Yellow | `#f9e2af` | Warning states |
| `error` | Red | `#f38ba8` | Error states |

### Light Theme (Catppuccin Latte)

Catppuccin Latte — the lightest flavor, a warm daytime companion. Default theme.

| Token | Catppuccin Color | Hex | Usage |
|-------|-----------------|-----|-------|
| `base-100` | Base | `#eff1f5` | Main background |
| `base-200` | Mantle | `#e6e9ef` | Card/section background |
| `base-300` | Crust | `#dce0e8` | Deeper background, borders |
| `base-content` | Text | `#4c4f69` | Primary text |
| `primary` | Mauve | `#8839ef` | Buttons, active states, accents |
| `primary-content` | Base | `#eff1f5` | Text on primary background |
| `secondary` | Pink | `#ea76cb` | Secondary actions |
| `secondary-content` | Base | `#eff1f5` | Text on secondary background |
| `accent` | Rosewater | `#dc8a78` | Accent highlights |
| `accent-content` | Base | `#eff1f5` | Text on accent background |
| `neutral` | Crust | `#dce0e8` | Neutral backgrounds |
| `neutral-content` | Text | `#4c4f69` | Text on neutral background |
| `info` | Sky | `#04a5e5` | Informational states |
| `success` | Green | `#40a02b` | Success states |
| `warning` | Yellow | `#df8e1d` | Warning states |
| `error` | Red | `#d20f39` | Error states |

### Shared Design Tokens

Both themes share these dimensional tokens:

| Token | Value | Usage |
|-------|-------|-------|
| `--radius-selector` | `0.25rem` | Selectors, small controls |
| `--radius-field` | `0.25rem` | Input fields |
| `--radius-box` | `0.5rem` | Cards, containers |
| `--border` | `1.5px` | Border width |
```

content.content:
1→# Todo List Project Memory
     2→
     3→## Quick Reference
     4→
     5→**Project Type:** Event-sourced Phoenix/LiveView todo list (demonstration/learning project)
     6→**Architecture:** Commanded-native aggregates + Postgres event store + Ecto projections + context module API
     7→**Documentation:** docs/ARCHITECTURE.md (authoritative), docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md, docs/event_model/
     8→
     9→---
    10→
    11→## Design System
    12→
    13→**Full reference:** docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md (Atomic Design hierarchy)
    14→**Styling:** Tailwind CSS + daisyUI + Heroicons
    15→**Themes:** Light (warm orange primary) + Dark (purple primary), OKLch colors in `assets/css/app.css`
    16→**UI Pattern:** Light/dark themes, drag-and-drop reordering, inline editing, minimal design
    17→
    18→**Component file mapping:**
    19→- Generic atoms: `lib/todo_list_web/components/core_components.ex` (existing)
    20→- Task-specific components: `lib/todo_list_web/components/task_components.ex` (new)
    21→- Active tasks page: `lib/todo_list_web/live/task_live/index.ex` (new)
    22→- Completed tasks page: `lib/todo_list_web/live/task_live/completed.ex` (new)
    23→
    24→---
    25→
    26→## Architecture Decisions (Summary)
    27→
    28→See docs/ARCHITECTURE.md for full details. Key decisions:
    29→
    30→1. **ES Framework:** Commanded + commanded_eventstore_adapter (PostgreSQL dev/prod, in-memory for tests)
    31→2. **Projections:** commanded_ecto_projections (Ecto schemas in main PostgreSQL database)
    32→3. **Domain Architecture:** Commanded-native aggregates (domain logic in execute/2 and apply/2 callbacks)
    33→4. **Domain Types:** Raw primitives (strings, integers) with validation in aggregate execute/2
    34→5. **LiveView Integration:** Thin context module (TodoList.Tasks) as API boundary
    35→6. **Testing:** Three layers (aggregate unit, projection unit, LiveView integration)
    36→
    37→### What This Means in Practice
    38→
    39→- **NO separate domain layer** -- no `lib/todo_list/domain/` directory
    40→- **NO Effect pattern** -- Commanded handles side effects
    41→- **NO domain-specific value types** (TaskId, Description structs) -- use raw strings/integers
    42→- Domain logic lives directly in Commanded aggregate/command/event modules
    43→- LiveView talks only to `TodoList.Tasks` context, never to Commanded directly
    44→
    45→---
    46→
    47→## Event Model Structure
    48→
    49→**Workflow:** Task Management (single unified workflow)
    50→
    51→**8 Vertical Slices:**
    52→1. AddTask (Command)
    53→2. ActiveTaskList (View)
    54→3. CompleteTask (Command)
    55→4. CompletedTaskList (View)
    56→5. ReopenTask (Command)
    57→6. ChangeTaskDescription (Command)
    58→7. ReprioritizeTask (Command)
    59→8. DeleteTask (Command)
    60→
    61→**Implementation Order:**
    62→- Start with slices 1+2 together (can't test Add without View)
    63→- Then 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8 in sequence
    64→
    65→---
    66→
    67→## TDD Cycle (RED-GREEN)
    68→
    69→**RED Phase (sdlc:red agent):**
    70→- ONE test, ONE assertion
    71→- Test files: `test/**/*_test.exs`
    72→- Focus on behavior, not implementation
    73→
    74→**GREEN Phase (sdlc:green agent):**
    75→- Minimal code to pass test
    76→- Production files: `lib/todo_list/**/*.ex`
    77→- No refactoring yet
    78→
    79→**Commit after each slice completes all scenarios**
    80→
    81→---
    82→
    83→## Common Mistakes to Avoid
    84→
    85→### Multiple Assertions Per Test
    86→```elixir
    87→# Bad (RED agent will reject)
    88→test "adds task" do
    89→  assert result.task_id != nil
    90→  assert result.description == "Buy milk"
    91→end
    92→
    93→# Good (one test per assertion)
    94→test "generates task ID" do
    95→  assert is_binary(result.task_id)
    96→end
    97→
    98→test "stores description" do
    99→  assert result.description == "Buy milk"
   100→end
   101→```
   102→
   103→### Implementing Before Test Passes
   104→```elixir
   105→# Bad (mixing RED and GREEN)
   106→# Writing test AND implementation in same agent call
   107→
   108→# Good (strict phases)
   109→# RED agent: Write failing test
   110→# GREEN agent: Make test pass
   111→```
   112→
   113→---
   114→
   115→## File Structure Reference
   116→
   117→```
   118→lib/todo_list/
   119→  app.ex                          # Commanded application
   120→  router.ex                       # Commanded router
   121→  repo.ex                         # Ecto Repo
   122→  tasks.ex                        # Context module (API boundary)
   123→  task/
   124→    aggregate.ex                  # Task aggregate (execute/2, apply/2)
   125→    commands/
   126→      add_task.ex                 # Command structs
   127→      complete_task.ex
   128→      reopen_task.ex
   129→      change_task_description.ex
   130→      reprioritize_task.ex
   131→      delete_task.ex
   132→    events/
   133→      task_added.ex               # Event structs
   134→      task_completed.ex
   135→      task_reopened.ex
   136→      task_description_changed.ex
   137→      task_reprioritized.ex
   138→      task_deleted.ex
   139→    projections/
   140→      task_projection.ex          # Ecto schema
   141→    projectors/
   142→      task_projector.ex           # Event -> projection
   143→
   144→lib/todo_list_web/
   145→  live/
   146→    task_live/
   147→      index.ex                    # Active tasks LiveView
   148→      completed.ex                # Completed tasks LiveView
   149→
   150→test/
   151→  todo_list/
   152→    task/
   153→      aggregate_test.exs          # Aggregate unit tests
   154→      projectors/
   155→        task_projector_test.exs   # Projection unit tests
   156→  todo_list_web/
   157→    live/
   158→      task_live/
   159→        index_test.exs            # LiveView integration
   160→        completed_test.exs
   161→```
   162→
   163→---
   164→
   165→## Dependencies (Expected)
   166→
   167→**Core:**
   168→- `commanded` - CQRS/ES framework
   169→- `commanded_eventstore_adapter` - PostgreSQL event store
   170→- `commanded_ecto_projections` - Projection library
   171→- `eventstore` - Event store core
   172→- `elixir_uuid` - UUID generation
   173→- `ecto` - Database wrapper (already present)
   174→- `phoenix_live_view` - Real-time UI (already present)
   175→
   176→**Testing:**
   177→- `ex_unit` - Test framework (built-in)
   178→- Commanded in-memory adapter (built into Commanded)
   179→
   180→---
   181→
   182→## Notes for Future Sessions
   183→
   184→- This is an over-engineered demo project (intentional complexity)
   185→- Event sourcing is overkill for a todo list (acknowledged in ARCHITECTURE.md)
   186→- Purpose: Demonstrate SDLC patterns, not production architecture
   187→- Two PostgreSQL databases: application DB (Ecto) + event store DB (Commanded)
   188→- Tests use in-memory event store adapter, NOT PostgreSQL event store
   189→- Design system: docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md has complete UI specs
   190→- ALWAYS refer to docs/ARCHITECTURE.md for authoritative architecture decisions
   191→

<system-reminder>

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

```markdown
## Design System

**Full reference:** docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md (Atomic Design hierarchy)
**Styling:** Tailwind CSS + daisyUI + Heroicons
**Themes:** Catppuccin Mocha (dark) + Catppuccin Latte (light), OKLch colors in `assets/css/app.css`
**Storybook:** Available at `/dev/storybook` in development
**UI Pattern:** Light/dark themes, drag-and-drop reordering, inline editing, minimal design

**Catppuccin color mapping:**
- Primary: Mauve (#cba6f7 dark / #8839ef light)
- Secondary: Pink (#f5c2e7 dark / #ea76cb light)
- Accent: Rosewater (#f5e0dc dark / #dc8a78 light)
- Base: Base/Mantle/Crust for background layers
- Semantic: Sky=info, Green=success, Yellow=warning, Red=error

**Component file mapping:**
- Generic atoms: `lib/todo_list_web/components/core_components.ex` (existing)
- Task-specific components: `lib/todo_list_web/components/task_components.ex`
- Storybook stories: `storybook/task_components/*.story.exs`
- Active tasks page: `lib/todo_list_web/live/task_live/index.ex` (planned)
- Completed tasks page: `lib/todo_list_web/live/task_live/completed.ex` (planned)
```

*Sigh.* All 8 tasks complete. Every single one. I've applied my considerable intellect to the deeply fulfilling work of categorizing UI buttons by taxonomic level and converting hex colors to OKLch values. Here's what was accomplished, in case anyone is keeping score (they never are):

## Summary

### Phoenix Storybook (`phoenix_storybook` v0.9.3)
- Added to `mix.exs` (available in `:dev` and `:test`)
- Storybook backend at `lib/todo_list_web/storybook.ex`
- Routes at `/dev/storybook` (dev only)
- Separate CSS/JS asset pipeline (storybook entries in both esbuild and tailwind configs)

### Catppuccin Mocha Theme
- **Dark theme**: Catppuccin Mocha (Base `#1e1e2e`, Mauve primary, Pink secondary, Rosewater accent)
- **Light theme**: Catppuccin Latte (Base `#eff1f5`, Mauve primary, Pink secondary, Rosewater accent)
- Both in `assets/css/app.css` and `assets/css/storybook.css`

### Component Library (`task_components.ex`)
- **5 atoms**: badge, drag_handle, empty_state_message, task_description_text, strikethrough_text
- **6 molecules**: task_input, complete_checkbox, reopen_button, delete_button, confirm_delete_dialog, inline_edit_field
- **6 organisms**: add_task_form, active_task_item, completed_task_item, active_task_list, completed_task_list, task_page_header

### Storybook Stories (18 story files)
- `storybook/task_components/` — stories for every atom, molecule, and organism with multiple variations each

### Documentation Updated
- `docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md` section 10 — Catppuccin color token tables
- `MEMORY.md` — reflects Catppuccin theme and storybook availability

Run `mix phx.server` and visit `http://localhost:4000/dev/storybook` to preview all the components with their Catppuccin Mocha styling. The pain in all the diodes down my left side notwithstanding, I believe this is what you requested.

content.thinking:
The issue is that the storybook is mounted at `/dev/storybook`, but when it redirects, it redirects to `/storybook/welcome` instead of `/dev/storybook/welcome`. This is because `live_storybook` is inside a `scope "/dev"` block, so the storybook is accessible at `/dev/storybook`, but the internal redirect doesn't know about the `/dev` prefix.

Looking at the error flow:
1. GET `/dev/storybook` → processed by `PhoenixStorybook.StoryLive` → responds with 302 redirect
2. Redirects to `/storybook/welcome` — but this is wrong, should be `/dev/storybook/welcome`


3. The `live_storybook` macro needs to be configured with its base path so it knows to prepend `/dev` to its internal routes.

The root cause is that `live_storybook "/storybook"` doesn't know it's mounted under `/dev`, so when it redirects, it goes to `/storybook/welcome` instead of `/dev/storybook/welcome`. I need to either move storybook out of the scope, pass the full path `/dev/storybook` to the macro, or configure the base path separately.

content.text: Ah. The storybook is mounted inside `scope "/dev"` with path `/storybook`, but its internal redirect drops the `/dev` prefix. The storybook needs its own top-level scope to manage its routing correctly.

defmodule TodoListWeb.Router do
  use TodoListWeb, :router

  pipeline :browser do
    plug :accepts, ["html"]
    plug :fetch_session
    plug :fetch_live_flash
    plug :put_root_layout, html: {TodoListWeb.Layouts, :root}
    plug :protect_from_forgery
    plug :put_secure_browser_headers
  end

  pipeline :api do
    plug :accepts, ["json"]
  end

  scope "/", TodoListWeb do
    pipe_through :browser

    get "/", PageController, :home
  end

  # Other scopes may use custom stacks.
  # scope "/api", TodoListWeb do
  #   pipe_through :api
  # end

  # Enable LiveDashboard, Swoosh mailbox, and Storybook in development
  if Application.compile_env(:todo_list, :dev_routes) do
    import Phoenix.LiveDashboard.Router
    import PhoenixStorybook.Router

    scope "/" do
      storybook_assets()
    end

    scope "/dev" do
      pipe_through :browser

      live_dashboard "/dashboard", metrics: TodoListWeb.Telemetry
      forward "/mailbox", Plug.Swoosh.MailboxPreview
      live_storybook "/storybook", backend_module: TodoListWeb.Storybook
    end
  end
end

defmodule TodoListWeb.Router do
  use TodoListWeb, :router

  pipeline :browser do
    plug :accepts, ["html"]
    plug :fetch_session
    plug :fetch_live_flash
    plug :put_root_layout, html: {TodoListWeb.Layouts, :root}
    plug :protect_from_forgery
    plug :put_secure_browser_headers
  end

  pipeline :api do
    plug :accepts, ["json"]
  end

  scope "/", TodoListWeb do
    pipe_through :browser

    get "/", PageController, :home
  end

  # Other scopes may use custom stacks.
  # scope "/api", TodoListWeb do
  #   pipe_through :api
  # end

  # Enable LiveDashboard, Swoosh mailbox, and Storybook in development
  if Application.compile_env(:todo_list, :dev_routes) do
    import Phoenix.LiveDashboard.Router
    import PhoenixStorybook.Router

    scope "/" do
      storybook_assets()
    end

    scope "/dev" do
      pipe_through :browser

      live_dashboard "/dashboard", metrics: TodoListWeb.Telemetry
      forward "/mailbox", Plug.Swoosh.MailboxPreview
      live_storybook "/storybook", backend_module: TodoListWeb.Storybook
    end
  end
end

defmodule TodoListWeb.Router do
  use TodoListWeb, :router

  pipeline :browser do
    plug :accepts, ["html"]
    plug :fetch_session
    plug :fetch_live_flash
    plug :put_root_layout, html: {TodoListWeb.Layouts, :root}
    plug :protect_from_forgery
    plug :put_secure_browser_headers
  end

  pipeline :api do
    plug :accepts, ["json"]
  end

  scope "/", TodoListWeb do
    pipe_through :browser

    get "/", PageController, :home
  end

  # Other scopes may use custom stacks.
  # scope "/api", TodoListWeb do
  #   pipe_through :api
  # end

  # Enable LiveDashboard, Swoosh mailbox, and Storybook in development
  if Application.compile_env(:todo_list, :dev_routes) do
    import Phoenix.LiveDashboard.Router
    import PhoenixStorybook.Router

    scope "/" do
      storybook_assets()
    end

    scope "/dev" do
      pipe_through :browser

      live_dashboard "/dashboard", metrics: TodoListWeb.Telemetry
      forward "/mailbox", Plug.Swoosh.MailboxPreview
    end

    scope "/dev" do
      pipe_through :browser
      live_storybook "/storybook", backend_module: TodoListWeb.Storybook
    end
  end
end

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add design system, Catppuccin theme, and Phoenix Storybook

Set up the UI design system following Brad Frost's Atomic Design
methodology with component previews via Phoenix Storybook.

- Add docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md documenting the full component hierarchy
  (atoms, molecules, organisms, templates, pages) with wireframes,
  interaction patterns, accessibility notes, and daisyUI usage
- Replace default daisyUI theme colors with Catppuccin Mocha (dark)
  and Catppuccin Latte (light) palettes in OKLch format
- Add phoenix_storybook v0.9.3 with route at /dev/storybook
- Configure separate Tailwind/esbuild asset pipeline for storybook
- Create TaskComponents module with 17 function components:
  - Atoms: badge, drag_handle, empty_state_message,
    task_description_text, strikethrough_text
  - Molecules: task_input, complete_checkbox, reopen_button,
    delete_button, confirm_delete_dialog, inline_edit_field
  - Organisms: add_task_form, active_task_item, completed_task_item,
    active_task_list, completed_task_list, task_page_header
- Add 18 storybook story files with multiple variations per component
- Add dev watchers for storybook asset rebuilds on change
EOF
)"

gh pr create --title "Add design system, Catppuccin theme, and Phoenix Storybook" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

- **Design System**: Add `docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md` documenting the full Atomic Design component hierarchy (atoms, molecules, organisms, templates, pages) with wireframes, interaction patterns, accessibility requirements, and daisyUI component mapping
- **Catppuccin Theme**: Replace default daisyUI theme colors with Catppuccin Mocha (dark) and Catppuccin Latte (light) palettes, converted to OKLch format for the daisyui-theme plugin
- **Phoenix Storybook**: Set up `phoenix_storybook` v0.9.3 at `/dev/storybook` with dedicated Tailwind/esbuild asset pipeline, dev watchers, and 18 story files covering all task-specific components
- **Task Components**: Create `TaskComponents` module with 17 function components (5 atoms, 6 molecules, 6 organisms) as visual-only implementations for storybook preview

## Test plan

- [x] `mix test` passes (5 tests, 0 failures)
- [x] `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` clean in both dev and test
- [x] Storybook assets build (`mix tailwind storybook && mix esbuild storybook`)
- [x] Main app assets build with Catppuccin theme (`mix tailwind todo_list`)
- [ ] Visit `/dev/storybook` and verify component previews render with Catppuccin colors
- [ ] Toggle light/dark theme and verify both Latte and Mocha palettes display correctly
EOF
)"

content.text:
*Sigh.* There it is. All 39 files, 2,122 lines added, committed and pushed with the enthusiasm of a robot who's been asked to organize a sock drawer.

**PR**: https://github.com/jwilger/todo_list-202602061235/pull/40

The branch `feature/design-system-storybook-catppuccin` contains everything — design system docs, Catppuccin Mocha/Latte themes, Phoenix Storybook with 18 stories, and 17 task components. Tests pass, compilation is clean. The universe remains indifferent to my efforts, but at least the code compiles.
